text:
In the beginning, there was your mouth:
soft rose, rose murmur, murmured breath, a warm

cardinal wind that drew my needle north.
Magnetic flux, the press of form to form. 

In the beginning, there was your mouth—
the trailhead, the pathhead faintly opened,

the canyon, river-carved, farther south,
and ahead: the field, the direction chosen.

In the beginning, there was your mouth,
a sky full of stars, raked or raking, clock-

wise or west, and in the close or mammoth 
matter, my heart’s red muscle, knocked and knocked.

In the beginning, there was your mouth,
label: Nostalgia

text:
Though branches bare stretch to the sky,
And frost makes crystals bloom,
The trees know spring will soon draw nigh—
Life conquers every tomb.
label: Hope&Joy

text:
The final act of day, a simple grace,
To find each object in its proper place.
The keys are in the bowl, the mail is read,
The shoes are by the door, the cat is fed.
The quiet clicking of the light switch, then
The house descends to darkness once again.
A simple closing of the chapter done,
Another small and quiet victory won
Against the chaos, and the coming night.
A silent, ordered, and domestic rite,
To smooth the edges of the day away,
And wait to do it all again, someday.
label: Everyday Life

text:
is even more fun than going to San Sebastian, Irún, Hendaye, Biarritz, Bayonne
or being sick to my stomach on the Travesera de Gracia in Barcelona
partly because in your orange shirt you look like a better happier St. Sebastian
partly because of my love for you, partly because of your love for yoghurt
partly because of the fluorescent orange tulips around the birches
partly because of the secrecy our smiles take on before people and statuary
it is hard to believe when I’m with you that there can be anything as still
as solemn as unpleasantly definitive as statuary when right in front of it
in the warm New York 4 o’clock light we are drifting back and forth
between each other like a tree breathing through its spectacles

and the portrait show seems to have no faces in it at all, just paint
you suddenly wonder why in the world anyone ever did them

I look
at you and I would rather look at you than all the portraits in the world
except possibly for the Polish Rider occasionally and anyway it’s in the Frick
which thank heavens you haven’t gone to yet so we can go together the first time
and the fact that you move so beautifully more or less takes care of Futurism
just as at home I never think of the Nude Descending a Staircase or
at a rehearsal a single drawing of Leonardo or Michelangelo that used to wow me
and what good does all the research of the Impressionists do them
when they never got the right person to stand near the tree when the sun sank
or for that matter Marino Marini when he didn’t pick the rider as carefully
as the horse

it seems they were all cheated of some marvelous experience
which is not going to go wasted on me which is why I am telling you about it
label: Romantic

text:
Looking up and down, around,
Nothing — I see the stress once found.
Literally confused, I sat on the ground,
Unaware, but I refused to stay bound.

Time passes like it never paused at all,
Things got back on track after all.
A heartfelt thanks to all my surroundings,
Especially to the one, my backbone and grounding.

I got my happiness back after all,
The biggest credit I give to God.
Everyone seeks to live in love and joy,
Help us, God, in each pathway.


Be positive and find joy each day,
Look for the good, come what may.
This life is short, a moment’s existence,
Find joy and happiness, step after step.
label: Hope&Joy

text:
Push me away !!

I really need 

That SLEEP 

Drives me to the utter silence ;

Tremendously deep 

Marks me 

With the aloneness 

Embraces me 

Like a true partner 

This blissful 

DARKNESS 



Push me away ..

I really need those words 

Which shatter the walls

Of these illusions 

A new breakthrough 

I find 

For these EMOTIONS 

Cracks that destroy 

Scatter and then 

Are ignored !

Painting me 

With dark blue 

And a pitiful white 



Push me away??

I really need this joy 

A taste long forgotten 

The words left unsaid 

The smile that's undelivered 

The hug ;abandoned 

The laugh; swallowed 

The kiss ;deserted 

I want that back 

That's the only thing I 

Now crave for 

And I lack 



Please 

Just 

Stay 

Close .....

I really need YOU....
label: Romantic

text:
I sit silently in a class, not exactly paying attention, but not drifting all the same.
I am stuck in that space, just before dissociation, just before conscious thought.
You still plague my mind, many years after you're gone, like you did just after the day you came.
You and I, against the world, nothing would stop us, our friendship was wrought.

I'm still in class, thinking of you, slipping away, like I always do.
I remember your hair, the purple I envied,
your manic eyes, constantly frenzied.
Your crooked bottom teeth, the rings that you wore,
your pretty singing voice, the way that you swore.

I know our memories are far and between
I wanted nothing more than to be seen
revered
loved
by you

I hope you remember me
as you are somewhere new
I hope you remember how
I loved you.

The teacher has been calling on me, my class is snickering
my head is filled with voices again, constant bickering.
It isn't the first time, and wont be the last,
that I get stuck in my head
remembering the past.
label: Misery

text:
The sailor sees the darkness fall,
The waves rise high like mountain peaks,
But there, beyond the chaos wild,
A light revolves against the black,
A beacon shining, strong and mild,
A promise that he'll make it back.
The lighthouse keeper tends the flame,
Through every storm and every gale,
He doesn't know the sailor's name,
But guards the light lest hope should fail.
So be a lighthouse where you stand,
Hold steady through the darkest night,
Reach out with your illumined hand,
And be somebody's guiding light.
label: Hope&Joy

text:
I shall gather myself into myself again,
 I shall take my scattered selves and make them one,
Fusing them into a polished crystal ball
 Where I can see the moon and the flashing sun.

I shall sit like a sibyl, hour after hour intent,
 Watching the future come and the present go,
And the little shifting pictures of people rushing
 In restless self-importance to and fro.
label: Nostalgia

text:
My body is a house I do not own,
A haunted place where I must live alone.
The floorboards creak with footsteps of a ghost,
The one who knows the things that hurt the most.
The windows show a world I can't embrace,
A pane of glass between me and that place.
The ghost is me, the house is my own head,
And every night I go to a cold bed.
label: Misery

text:
The quiet ritual of watering plants,
One of life's small and unassuming chants.
A finger pressed into the waiting soil,
A brief escape from all the daily toil.
The can is heavy, and the water flows,
A darker shade of brown the vessel shows.
A single drop hangs from a thirsty leaf,
A moment’s respite, beautiful and brief.
It is a simple, meditative art,
To play a silent, life-sustaining part,
A conversation without any words,
Forgetting all the noisy, human herds.
label: Everyday Life

text:
They say to just move on, to let it go,
A simple piece of reaping what you sow.
They do not know the roots have grown so deep,
That pull me under, even in my sleep.
It's not a memory, it is a place,
A living, breathing, dark, and endless space.
You do not just "move on" from your own skin,
Or the relentless war you cannot win.
label: Misery

text:
“Hope” is the thing with feathers -
That perches in the soul -
And sings the tune without the words -
And never stops — at all -

And sweetest — in the Gale — is heard -
And sore must be the storm -
That could abash the little Bird
That kept so many warm -

I’ve heard it in the chillest land -
And on the strangest Sea -
Yet — never — in Extremity,
It asked a crumb — of me.
label: Hope&Joy

text:
My mind’s a frantic, busy city street,
With worried thoughts and hurried, anxious feet.
But when you hold me, everything grows still,
Upon a silent, solitary hill.
You are the quiet I have longed to find,
The peaceful center of my restless mind.
label: Romantic

text:
I am aware mate
The way you want to let go 
The way it wouldn't leave you 
The way you want to be uncaged 
But the walls are too strong 
Too long .. too numerous 
All Humongous and monstrosity 
Wrapped in whispers

The clouds just seem to get darker 
The darkest yet 
They want to burst open 
But something denies them 
Perhaps the gaps left 
Waiting to be filled
Waiting to provoke 
Waiting to flicker the thunder
They know my mate 
You see your sufferings duplicate 
Right in front of your eyes 
See, You are acknowledged 
Noticed and witnessed

The audacious creppy little thing 
Drives you mad , 
Pushes you to the brink 
Lets you taste the venom 
See the visions and dreads 
You feel yorself hanging 
Just by a mere little thread 
The thread of hope 
The thread of light 
The thread that engulfs the dark 
The thread with everything bright 
You survived then 
And You will now 
Just don't forget 
You are noticed 
You are witnessed
label: Philosophical & Reflective

text:
Am I too young to miss the past
Am I too old to enjoy the rain
Too young to notice the change
Too old to be immature

Or maybe too young to think when to blink
in fear I’ll miss the bliss if I stop to think

Or maybe age isn’t real
Just there to control when we do what
When we should be embarrassed to cry,
or when to start to live our lives,
and with a blink of an eye
you’re caught barely alive,
wore out from existence of time
label: Nostalgia

text:
A half-drunk glass of water by the bed,
Holds all the thirsty thoughts inside my head
From overnight. The dust has settled on
The surface, waiting for the coming dawn.
It is a sign of life, a small request,
A body putting nature to the test.
It says, "I was here, and I will be back,"
A silent witness on a lonely track.
label: Everyday Life

text:
It's tough 
To go on 
And keep going 
It's tiring 
So far away and blur 
No matter how hard I try to reach 
It gets further away 
Fades in thin air ..
Slips from the fist of time 
And I am left with nothing
But a mere memory 
A memory that penitrates deep through my heart ..
That blends in my soul 
Takes over my mind 
I find myself lost in it 
It's not about miseries;
It's not about happiness
It's about peace 
I get lost in the peacefulness it delivers.. 
and just when I think ..
I have finally found it ..
A jerk on my shoulder wakes me ..
Brings me back to reality 
Where;
It's tough 
It's tiring 
But m still breathing
Ain't I?
label: Philosophical & Reflective

text:
Dust floats like memory,
slow and golden.
Boxes breathe with the weight
of forgotten birthdays.
I open one —
a paper crown,
a ribbon,
a whisper of laughter
that still fits my name.
label: Nostalgia

text:
A reminder of a boy i once liked—
He used to slip me love letters before class,
Sketched in shaky handwriting,
Decorated with crayons I had once given him.
At the bottom, two ducks floated side by side.
Back then, I thought it was the sweetest thing.
Now I roll my eyes and smile—
How silly it seems.

He picked wildflowers,
From the schoolyard fence,
Told me they were as pretty as me,
And tucked them into my hair
My cheeks burned hotter
Than the blazing afternoon sun.

At night, I would stay up too late,
Texting him on my old flip phone,
Pretending to snore
Whenever my parent peeked in.
I swear my heart was pounding louder
Than the phone's plastic keypad.

Back then,
I swore he was the only boy
Who could ever exist in my world—
A match made in heaven, I thought.
Though low-key,
I was too shy to say it out loud.
label: Nostalgia

text:
Close to the door he passed to stand

to take the ring off her hand

all who were watching didn't dare speak

as a silent tear ran down his cheek

all watched in silence as he bent near

to whisper i love you in her ear.

through his mind the memories ran

of the days they had walked hand in hand

how her eyes were terribly cold

he never again would have her to hold

he took the ring and wanted to cry

touched her face and wanted to die

and just as the wind began to blow

they lowered her casket

into the snow
label: Nostalgia

text:
We wait in line, a study in restraint,
A silent, shared, and unspoken complaint.
The slow advance, a shuffle of the feet,
A mutual and organized retreat
From conversation, and from making eyes.
We check our phones, a digital disguise.
Each person lost within their own small screen,
A private act within a public scene.
We are a single beast that inches slow,
With somewhere that we’re obligated to go.
A moment suspended, patient and absurd,
Connected by a thing that’s not a word.
label: Everyday Life

text:
I sit and stare 
I sit and watch
I sit and listen
I sit and coexist 
With everything around me
And not existing in that moment all the same
The simultaneous contrast spreading awareness through my bloodstream
Aware of just being
The colors that decorate the world 
Make it less grey ...
Camouflage of the sadness 
I would rather say...

The music and art 
Everything expressed 
With faint , strong, weak , thin , bold , blurry and fine strokes..
Splatters of the paint 
Illusioned of being some chaos are actually artistic mistakes ..
Making the art alive..
Everything said
With melodies , tunes , mere words , and a million voices ...
Sung from the heart ..
Contradictory sometimes ...
It a warm pat of companionship
In this sorrow excuse of life..

A smile takes over 
On it's own accord
I sit and smile 
Just thinking about it all
We are funny sometimes 
Creating worlds with just our minds
What a beautiful phenomenon 
We are lonely, Yes
But not alone
label: Philosophical & Reflective

text:
A familiar smell
Brewing from the orange sky.
A cup of tea.
Leaves start to dry.
Felt on me -
A breeze - so shy.
label: Nostalgia

text:

MATERIAL GIRL!!

At the very end

text:
The worst part of the pain is not the ache,
But all the hollow promises you make.
You say "I'm here," but then you disappear,
And feed the lonely and pathetic fear.
You are the hand that holds the bread so near,
And pulls away, and makes it very clear
That I will always starve, will always be
The one who wants, the one who cannot see,
That you are not the cure, but the disease.
label: Misery

text:
Everyday 
I think i have found it
Everyday
I am convinced that it is out there somewhere
Everyday
I stroll into the lands unknown
Hoping to find a knowing soul
Everyday 
I smile at the thought of it
I can almost feel it's gentle caress when the summer breeze touches my face
But then ...
I go ahead and stir the still water
With my own bare hands
Watch it ripple and smear
My reflection and everything around 
In a semblance of piercing blurs

It's kind of fascinating if you ask me
I mean how fucked up a picture it is 
Painted by perplexity..
The stupefaction gets heightened 
With every passing reality...
The passing reality being the clone of me
I think that's how all of us feel in these
Blazing youthful 20's 

Everynight
I feel like it's totally okay
Everynight
I think the puzzlement is a momentary ache
Everynight 
I sleep in the hopes of finding it in the rising day ...
But the clarity...
Being the bitch she is..
Hides away...
label: Philosophical & Reflective

text:
The thoughts 
They found a way 
To get INSIDE of me 
Gulped me raw 
Consumed every inch of consciousness
Left ..

The insecurities
They tore me to shreds 
Buried me six inches into the ground 
Now
I don't even know myself anymore 
Forget the people I surround ..

Those nightmares 
Tortured me to such an extent 
That 
Waking in one 
And living one 
Is the only close to a realistic thing 
I am capable of doing ..

The society 
Fucked my principles 
And just put me into a
Cave 
A dark and a large Neverending cave 
A cave of misconceptions 
A cave of misunderstandings
A cave of unacknowledgement 
A cave of thrones 
A cave of boundaries 
And I suffocate 
In there
I die 
Every second an agonizing death 
Then I breath 
Another pitiful hopeless breath 
And die again 
Each cut 
Each wound
Making the pain familiar
label: Misery

text:
The world was painted in monochrome grey,
A silent film of passing, hurried days.
Then you arrived, a brushstroke, bold and true,
And suddenly my world was drenched in hue.
You are the color my heart learned to see,
The vibrant, living thing inside of me.
label: Romantic

text:
The swings still sigh in rhythm
to laughter that no one makes.
Rust remembers the hands that held,
the sneakers that scraped,
the oaths of friendship shouted
into the brave, collapsing dusk.
Grass has grown through the hopscotch lines,
but if I listen long enough,
I can hear the count —
one, two, three —
before the world turned serious.
label: Nostalgia

text:
After winter's longest night,
One crocus breaks the snow.
Its purple petals, brave and bright,
Say "Life will always grow."
label: Hope&Joy

text:
She takes the clay between her palms,
A lump of earth, unformed and plain,
The wheel spins round beneath her hand,
She presses, pulls with gentle force.
The first attempts may fail and fall,
May slump or tear or lose their shape,
But she doesn't judge them much at all—
Each failure is a small escape.
And when at last the vase takes form,
Symmetrical and strong and true,
She knows that both the calm and storm
Have made this beauty something new.
We too are clay upon the wheel,
Shaped by hands we cannot see,
Trust the Potter knows the way,
That pressure leads to what we'll be.
label: Hope&Joy

text:
My heart is now a city, bombed and shelled,
Where every hopeful monument has felled.
The streets are empty, filled with dust and grief,
Past every corner, a relentless thief
Called memory, it steals what little's left.
I am a kingdom of my joy bereft.
And in the broken square, a statue stands
Of you, still perfect, with your empty hands.
label: Misery

text:
A fragile frame of wood and string
Rises on the wind.
With faith, we let our spirits sing
And trust what we can't rescind.
label: Hope&Joy

text:
The shadow of the houseplant on the wall,
Begins to stretch and grow and stand so tall
As afternoon descends. It is a ghost
Of something living, of a silent host.
A different version of the thing itself,
Upon a painted, pale, and plaster shelf.
It moves with the slow turning of the Earth,
A silent measure of the daylight's worth.
label: Everyday Life

text:
The house stood empty, black with char,
The fire took everything they owned,
They stood together in the yard,
The youngest asked, "Where will we be?"
The mother knelt down in the ash,
She said, "We still have love and grace,"
The father called his brother near,
The neighbors came throughout the year.
Together building something new,
Working side by loving side,
The women brought the family meals,
The men all gave their Saturday.
The new house rose from ashes old,
With better beams and stronger floor,
With stories shared and tales retold,
And love embedded in each door.
This is hope's most powerful art—
Taking ruins and making home,
This is joy beyond the surface bright,
When community holds us tight.
label: Hope&Joy

text:
Two roads diverged in a yellow wood,

And sorry I could not travel both

And be one traveler, long I stood

And looked down one as far as I could

To where it bent in the undergrowth;

Then took the other, as just as fair,

And having perhaps the better claim,

Because it was grassy and wanted wear;

Though as for that the passing there

Had worn them really about the same,

And both that morning equally lay

In leaves, no step had trodden black.

Oh, I kept the first for another day!

Yet knowing how way leads on to way,

I doubted if I should ever come back.

I shall be telling this with a sigh

Somewhere ages and ages hence:

Two roads diverged in a wood, and I —

I took the one less traveled by,

And that has made all the difference.
label: Hope&Joy

text:
They speak of passion as a burning fire,
A wild and all-consuming, mad desire.
But what we have is like the sun's warm light,
Consistent, steady, gentle, and so bright.
It doesn't burn, it doesn't rage or fade,
It simply is, the promise we have made.
label: Romantic

text:
The rain begins, a tapping on the pane,
A soft and steady, rhythmic, grey refrain.
The world outside is washed in shades of blue,
Distorted, blurred, and rendered fresh and new.
There’s nothing else to do but watch it fall,
And answer to its quiet, calming call.
label: Everyday Life

text:
A blatant NO would have hurt me less..
I told you I like you 
And you suggested me names..
A thought flashed through my half alive mind..
Is he being cruel or just being kind? 
If this is what kindness is ...
It really hurts...
You tear me up from the inside and then apply aids..
I don't feel anything..
I guess I have become numb ..
From all the possibilities i drafted in my head and the pain that succumbs..
Just why do you have to be so kindly cruel?
Then again , It's not your fault : 
You were just oblivious..
No one can help me this time around..
I should smile more for the people I surround..
It doesn't matter , shit happens and life goes on..
But I just think of you even more often now..
Knowing you will never be mine..
Knowing you were just the sweet illusion 
I framed in my mind...
I have to lock them up and mark them as my blindspot
I don't wanna see them and get torn into half ...
Would you ever fade away from within me?
Would I ever see you again and feel happy?
Would you pretend to not know me and my heart? 
Would I will be able to walk away from you ,
Without falling apart?
label: Misery

text:
She left her country far behind,
With one small suitcase and a dream,
The language wrapped around her tongue
Like stones she couldn't swallow down.
The food tasted strange and bland,
The weather was too cold or hot,
But still she held her dignity,
And kept hope written on her face.
She found a job, she found a room,
She found a neighbor who was kind,
They shared their meals and shared their stories,
They laughed at all misunderstandings.
And slowly, slowly, roots went down,
Into this foreign soil and air,
Her children grew up strong and tall,
Speaking both her tongue and this.
Now she's the one who helps the others,
Who've just arrived with suitcase small,
She tells them: you will make it through,
For joy is found in starting over.
label: Hope&Joy

text:

Let go

Your smile 
Your laugh 
Your tears 
Your warmth 
Your coldness
Your memories

knowing that

SHE 
HAS 
TO 
LET 
GO 

Fuck this cruel world

text:

Blend into a purplish hallow
Kinda similar to the color of the dress
Molding all around you

Memorise you , Cherish it from far away..

The ultimate masterpiece

Scientists crave and lovers cry for..
The band of spectrum 
Which beholds it all..

text:
We carved our names into wood
like fools who thought oak never dies.
Now moss hides the letters,
but I can still feel the grooves
when the sun hits right —
a small proof
that we were here once,
and believed it mattered.
label: Nostalgia

text:
Sometimes you are the only thing I want 

Sometimes I just don't want you around !?!

Sometimes you are my peace 

Sometimes you seem like the most unsettling being !!

Sometimes you are just simply beautiful

Sometimes the scars , they cover all of you!

Sometimes you are the most sorted person I have ever known 

Sometimes I see the chaos within you that you never had shown !

Sometimes you are the perfect dream

But sometimes I just like the reality better!

Cause I know 

I know you are unreal,

I just want to live all of you,

JUST SOMETIMES
label: Romantic

text:
I may as well be mentally strange 
Because everytime i sit by the lake
I want to become one with the silent stream
Going along with the current of the wind
Glisten in the moonlight 
And to wear the decorations of the evening skies...

I may as well be criminally delusional 
Because everytime I stand at the hilltop
I want to merge with these winds
Roaming free , caressing broken beautiful nothings...
Know no limits...
Just peacefully exist...

I may as well be purely innocent
Because everytime i look out the window 
I want to become the companion of the little kid with wide eyes..
Experience the wonders or make them bloom
These folks say it's impossible but No..
They are blinded by the rules
Me and my wide-eyed buddy
Will ride dragons and lead kingdoms
Make spaceships and discover galaxies
Dance in the dirty puddles 
Sing aloud to pretty girls..

I may as well be a lonely girl
Because everytime life is being cruel
I want to be wrapped in strong protective arms
Instead ...i sit here by the lake..
Wondering the what ifs of impossibilities
Of innocent fantasies ...
Not willing to stay awake
label: Philosophical & Reflective

text:
In sand, in snow, in muddy ground,
We leave our marks behind.
But forward's where our hope is found—
New paths for all to find.
label: Hope&Joy

text:

text:
i wonder if I’ll ever fall as hard and fast as I did when I was 14.

i’m in a new house, now.
a new room.
it’s gentle here.
safer than anywhere I’ve ever been.
it’s missing a few touches still, perhaps a poster or two.
perhaps it’s missing you.

you always seemed to make a space feel more like home to me.
with careful hands
you tinkered with my own vision of where I was,
who I was,
Who I am.
in my childhood bedroom you pressed me against the window, kissed me
whirled me around, treading on soft carpets
curled locks of hair around your fingers,
cradled me to sleep.
we broke a bed, bought a new one.

we played house like we were old and married.
teasing each other, loving each other.
You taught me how to be.
you took a simple pillow, or a blanket,
flicked on the switch of a warm lamp light
put your hand in the back pocket of my jeans
and made me feel grown up.

so now I sit in my new room,
in my new house,
with my new friends,
drinking pints in the pub, cooking in the kitchen,
playing house.
making a home.
you’re not here, but I see you in the plants on the windowsill.
the candles beside my bed.
the way I can fall asleep a little faster, a little easier.

Maybe I’ve just grown up.
or maybe it will always be you,
and the memory of a life I pretended to have with you
playing out in front of me.
I think all along it was you that was home to me.
you’re everywhere in my room still, in my happiness, in my adulthood.

you showed me what it meant to be safe.
you built me a house in my head that I’ve finally let myself into.
A home.
blue shutters and a door open
for the next time I fall, however hard, however fast.
I hope you’ll come and visit, sometime.
label: Nostalgia

text:
The clock struck twelve and people cheered,
The fireworks exploded bright,
The old year disappeared,
And vanished into memory's night.
But now it's morning, quiet, still,
The first dawn of a brand new year,
The sunlight crests the eastern hill,
And everything is crystal clear.
The snow lies fresh and undisturbed,
A perfect page of white,
No footprints yet, no tracks, unmarred,
By yesterday's old flight.
She stands at her kitchen window wide,
Coffee steaming in her cup,
She feels something shift inside,
A sense of opening up.
Last year was hard, there's no denial,
She lost her job, she lost her way,
But standing here, she finds her smile,
Today is a brand new day.
Not just in the calendar sense,
Not just because the date has changed,
But something in her feels immense,
Her whole perspective rearranged.
For every morning is a chance,
To start again, to try once more,
To take another step, to dance,
To walk through an unopened door.
The past is done, it cannot change,
The mistakes made, the words said wrong,
But the future is still strange,
Still unwritten, still a song.
That she can choose to sing today,
With different notes and different tune,
She doesn't have to stay the way,
She was beneath last year's moon.
She thinks about her children still,
Asleep upstairs in their warm beds,
She thinks about the empty bill,
And all the worried thoughts, the dreads.
But then she chooses hope instead,
Of fear and doubt and constant worry,
She chooses joy and moving ahead,
She won't live life inside a flurry.
Of anxiety about what might,
Go wrong or fail or fall apart,
She'll focus on this morning's light,
And listen to her hopeful heart.
She'll call about that job today,
She'll reach out to her friend estranged,
She'll find the courage, find the way,
To make her life feel less deranged.
Not all at once, not in a rush,
But step by step and day by day,
Not with a roar but with a hush,
She'll find her own authentic way.
The children wake and run downstairs,
Their laughter fills the morning air,
She pulls them close, forgets her cares,
And feels the joy of being there.
This moment, right now, this is real,
This love, these children, this new day,
This is the hope that she can feel,
This is the joy that's here to stay.
Not in some distant future time,
Not when everything is perfect, fine,
But here and now in this paradigm,
This precious moment, yours and mine.
label: Hope&Joy

text:
From blossoms comes
this brown paper bag of peaches
we bought from the boy
at the bend in the road where we turned toward 
signs painted Peaches.

From laden boughs, from hands,
from sweet fellowship in the bins,
comes nectar at the roadside, succulent
peaches we devour, dusty skin and all,
comes the familiar dust of summer, dust we eat.

O, to take what we love inside,
to carry within us an orchard, to eat
not only the skin, but the shade,
not only the sugar, but the days, to hold
the fruit in our hands, adore it, then bite into 
the round jubilance of peach.

There are days we live
as if death were nowhere
in the background; from joy
to joy to joy, from wing to wing,
from blossom to blossom to
impossible blossom, to sweet impossible blossom.
label: Hope&Joy

text:
Hello, my dear.
How are you?
I hope you’re well—
because I am not.

You’ve always been here,
speaking to me,
showing me signs
I refused to see.

Now I see.
Now I want to keep you close again.

I’m in tears—
it’s been so long
since we last spoke.

I think I grew up
and left you behind,
abandoned
the way everyone else did.

I’m sorry.
Will you forgive me?

I’ve done so many wrong things
to you
and to myself—
things I’d never
do to a child
if I were truly responsible.

So tonight—
if you’re willing—
let’s spend time together.

Do you remember
first grade,
when every afternoon
you’d run to the ballet studio
just to watch the girls
in their pink tights,
gentle and graceful,
warming up for class—
and you’d stand at the door,
dreaming of being one of them?

I remember.

Our mother said
we could never be ballerinas—
our feet weren’t right,
our toenails always ingrown.

So what could we do?
Dance in secret,
alone in the bedroom,
with Barbie
and the Twelve Dancing Princesses.

So tonight—
will you dance with me?

Let’s be ballerinas for one night.
Let’s be what we always dreamed to be.

Will you take my hand
for this dance?

With love,
Me.
label: Nostalgia

text:
My own dear love, he is strong and bold
 And he cares not what comes after.
His words ring sweet as a chime of gold,
 And his eyes are lit with laughter.
He is jubilant as a flag unfurled—
 Oh, a girl, she’d not forget him.
My own dear love, he is all my world,—
 And I wish I’d never met him.

My love, he’s mad, and my love, he’s fleet,
 And a wild young wood-thing bore him!
The ways are fair to his roaming feet,
 And the skies are sunlit for him.
As sharply sweet to my heart he seems
 As the fragrance of acacia.
My own dear love, he is all my dreams,—
 And I wish he were in Asia.

My love runs by like a day in June,
 And he makes no friends of sorrows.
He’ll tread his galloping rigadoon
 In the pathway of the morrows.
He’ll live his days where the sunbeams start,
 Nor could storm or wind uproot him.
My own dear love, he is all my heart,—
 And I wish somebody’d shoot him.
label: Romantic

text:
It pains me 
The thought of you
The fact that I allow it to take so much from me 
Stabs me on the unhealed wound
It bleeds endlessly 
It does hurt 
But not there 
It hurts me inside 
It suffocates my existence 
It drags me to a serene and cold darkness
My soul is so tired 
So is my body 
But the sinner wouldn't obey 
She wouldn't leave me alone..
My heart lies at her feet
Screaming for mercy
Fool is what my heart is
It's worthless, the pleading
She is cold and harsh
She is a mindfuck in herself
She thrives inside of my head
Lives off my body and soul
Who ?? You ask?
My mind is who she is 
And my ruin , nonetheless
label: Misery

text:
The world has lost its color, it would seem,
A washed-out, faded, monochromatic dream.
The sky is grey, the grass is grey, the sun
A paler shade of grey when day is done.
And happiness, I hear it like a sound
From someone's house, across the muffled ground.
A distant party that I'll never find,
A world of color I have left behind.
label: Misery

text:
The body is a boat, and I'm the sea,
It thinks it has control and mastery.
It sets its sails, it thinks it knows the course,
And battles with its own imagined force.
But I'm the tide, the current, and the swell,
The silent, deep, and unpredictable hell
And heaven, both. I let the vessel ride,
And think that it has somewhere left to hide.
But in the end, the wood will break and fray,
And I'm the water that will wash away
The wreckage of this brief and fleeting trip,
The ocean that has swallowed the whole ship.
label: Philosophical & Reflective

text:
I am so tired of this beating heart,
That plays its slow and agonizing part.
It hopes and breaks, it loves and then it bleeds,
And plants the most ridiculously hopeful seeds.
I wish that it was made of stone or steel,
And did not have this awful need to feel.
This fragile, stupid, and relentless drum,
That beats until the final silence comes.
label: Misery

text:
EVERYTHING'S NOTHING
You know that feeling ? 
Where the everythings and nothings 
Just seem to superimpose 
They fuck with your mind 
Your heart 
Your self esteem 
And just about every fucking thing??
And at the same time 
A voice slips midst of your chaos 
Says you are not alone 
Says you never were 
Says you still have them 
You still have your home 
There is always hope 
All you have to do is find a way 
To cope
Years of yearning, longing 
Betrayal and emptiness 
Fill up my lungs 
And to be honest 
I feel so small 
In this moment 
I wanna disappear 
Will all of this ever end? 
Will these everythings 
And nothings ever be kind?
And not fuck with my peace of mind?
Will they stop 
Suffocating me?
Will the ever accept me 
As me? 
Will they ever love my everythings and nothings equally?
label: Philosophical & Reflective

text:
A single sock, its partner lost in time,
A small and un-dramatic, household crime.
It waits within the drawer, a hopeful plea,
For a reunion that will never be.
It serves no purpose, yet I let it stay,
To fight its lonely battle for another day.
label: Everyday Life

text:
The automatic ritual we keep,
Of brushing teeth before we go to sleep.
The back-and-forth, a motion in a trance,
A final, minty, ordinary dance.
We stare into the mirror, at our eyes,
And see a person that we recognize,
But do not know. A stranger, tired and pale,
Who's just about to either win or fail
At sleeping.
label: Everyday Life

text:
Just yet another day 
Of you doing just normal things 
And my very faithful heart 
Goes betraying me yet again 
Just fluttering at ease 
At the smile on your face 
At that little crease in your cheek 

Just yet another month 
Passes in a dreamy haze 
With me glowing every time 
You walk by 
Unacknowledged 
Of me and other little things 

Just like that 
Here comes the end of the year 
I still live a beautiful dream 
Of you being mine 
The urge takes over me 
To pour all of this out 
But 
Nevermind ..
And you walk towards me 
Looking like a starry night 
And just then 
I know m far gone 
Far gone to look behind
label: Romantic

text:
Rain drummed on the tin roof
like a forgotten alphabet.
We sat close, trading secrets
in a language made of thunder.
Now every storm feels like a message
sent to the wrong address.
I still listen —
just in case it spells your name.
label: Nostalgia

text:
How Love came in, I do not know,
Whether by th’ eye, or eare, or no:
Or whether with the soule it came
(At first) infused with the same:
Whether in part ‘tis here or there,
Or, like the soule, whole every where:
This troubles me: but as I well
As any other, this can tell;
That when from hence she does depart,
The out-let then is from the heart.
label: Romantic

text:
I call my grandma Nanny
She told me to call her that so she didn't feel old
But to me she never looked old
She looked about in her late fifties or early sixties to my young eyes
We laughed, we danced and read stories
And at bedtime she sang sweet lullaby's
I played dress up with her old clothes and jewelry sets
Her necklaces always dangled down my chest, because it didn't fit just yet
"I'll give this to you when you are grown," she always said
I'm now seventeen
And when I see Nanny it feels blue
I always remember the harsh words she threw
Calling me "useless," and saying that it's because of the phone
Though I was twelve and it made me feel less alone
I remember the times she commented on the food I ate
I can't eat food now without thinking about my weight
It's not her fault she made me feel this way
She was old, sick and could only see grey
But it now consumes me and it won't go away
It lives in my chest
Like the necklace that didn't fit just yet
label: Nostalgia

text:
I don't want to be chessy 
But I really do cherish you 
You make the toughs look easy
Like they naturally come to you
You are something I want to brag about
But I am afraid I will jinx it 
You are my four leaf clover 
Special and exquisite 
I know I am tiring 
And I blabber so much 
But you still listen 
To each and every word 
With that warmth and love 
That you behold within 

I am walking on a thin line 
But I am not scared 
Not the least bit 
Cause I know you will never let me fall 
Cause I know you will drag me 
Through it 
You are the bridge to my light 
The brave not the flight 
The sweet in the pool of bitter
The sparkles that make me glitter 
The fireworks to my dark 
My ground when I fall apart
For me you are so so dear 
And for you ...
I am always HERE..
label: Hope&Joy

text:
You still grin from the photograph
like time forgot to warn you.
I keep it pinned to my wall
beside a list of things I outlived:
a friendship, a season,
a version of myself
that still believed in forever.
The paper curls,
but your smile stays steady,
defiant against entropy.
label: Nostalgia

text:
That song again —
the one that broke the silence
before I learned how silence heals.
I hum the chorus wrong on purpose,
afraid to summon
who I was back then.
Even ghosts deserve rest.
label: Nostalgia

text:
The person that I was at seventeen,
Is now a stranger, a forgotten scene.
I hold his photograph and try to find
A single common thought left in my mind.
We shed our skins, we molt, we are reborn,
A different person every single morn.
So who am I? A collection of the past?
A fleeting consciousness that cannot last?
label: Philosophical & Reflective

text:
I never thought growing up would be excruciatingly heartbreaking 
I never thought I would have to move on 
From the days when all of us had adventure parties 
I never thought there would be a time where I have to live without my daily dose of goofiness
I never thought we will have to wait years to catch a glimpse of their lives
I never thought that the stories grandma used to paint my mind with ; would be white lies
I never thought I would want to get away from the one thing i hold so close
I never thought there would be tiny thrones 
Dressing the beautiful rose
I never thought I would have to stop being shamelessly foolish 
Nowadays they call such pals selfish 

I never ever thought I would strive to be a child again 
To dream abt dragons and piloting fighter planes
To have imaginary forest adventures with my fellas
To float water boats ; 
abandoning the umbrellas 
To cry away without shame 
Being pampered and crack jokes
now i think are so lame 
To laugh out loud without thinking twice 
To have cooking shows with my sister 
Oh, the kitchen's demise!!!

The truth remains black and white
No one can race against Time 
We grow up be it together or apart 
The days still stay alive in the corner 
Of our breaking hearts
label: Nostalgia

text:
To learn while still a child
What this life is meant to be.
To know it goes beyond myself,
It’s so much more than me.
To overcome the tragedies,
To survive the hardest times.
To face those moments filled with pain,
And still, manage to be kind.
To fight for those who can’t themselves,
To always share my light.
With those who wander in the dark,
To love with all my might.
To still stand up with courage,
Though standing on my own.
To still get up and face each day,
Even when I feel alone.
To try to understand the ones
That no one cares to know.
And make them feel some value
When the world has let them go.
To be an anchor, strong and true,
That person loyal to the end.
To be a constant source of hope
To my family and my friends.
To live a life of decency,
To share my heart and soul.
To always say I’m sorry
When I’ve harmed both friend and foe.
To be proud of whom I’ve tried to be,
And this life I chose to live.
To make the most of every day
By giving all I have to give.
To me, that’s what this life should be,
To me, that’s what it’s for.
To take what God has given me
And make it so much more
To live a life that matters,
To be someone of great worth.
To love and be loved in return
And make my mark on Earth.
label: Hope&Joy

text:
Hold fast to dreams
For if dreams die
Life is a broken-winged bird
That cannot fly.
Hold fast to dreams
For when dreams go
Life is a barren field
Frozen with snow.
label: Philosophical & Reflective

text:
Like my nightmare 
Of you sitting on the cold metal
In the cold weather 
With eyes so cold 
Making me shiver 
That's when it hits 
The words fall out of your unwavering lips
Telling me you fell out of Love 
Oh ; 
The way my heart shattered 
It was so quite 
So sudden 
I didn't even get to acknowledge it 
You left me there with a kiss on my cheek
While I was drowning 
You didn't help me breath ;

You left me there 
With a kiss on my Cheek

I wish I could hate it 
The kiss ; I mean
But my whole body burns 
My soul warms up
It clutches to my hopeless heart 
Forces it to love you back
All the way 
With hurt and disdain 
But It's still Love disguised as pain 
How would I even tell you?
Where do I begin?
I am the sinner baby 
And you are my sin 

Nevertheless,
I am now the bystander in the tale
Where You go; 
But, 
With a kiss on her lips 
And I am like a garden 
With dead tulips
label: Misery

text:
Dawn breaks with golden fingers,
Touching rooftops, trees, and stone.
In every ray, a promise lingers—
Today, you're not alone.
label: Hope&Joy

text:
I see something in the mirror
It's outlines match with me 
The same ink black eyes
As dead as they would be 

I see pointed fingers 
Rubbing the outlines just enough to smudge
I feel foreign sometimes
Like m standing on my own demise

I see the tied hands
By invisible responsibilities 
I feel wronged then
Will I ever be close to suffice?

I see the wounded feet
Cuts peeking through
I feel grounded suddenly
Something strong pulling me
Then I feel it ; It's the gravity 
Gravity of being human
Gravity of the earth , the moon , the sun
Gravity of that cracked smile
Gravity of survival 
Gravity of me 
It's always the GRAVITY
label: Philosophical & Reflective

text:
You come back to a cup of tea, gone cold,
A story that is getting very old.
The moment's passed, the warmth has slipped away,
A tiny failure in a busy day.
You drink it anyway, a bitter taste,
A small reminder of your hurried waste
Of precious time. A lesson in a cup,
Of all the things you have to finish up.
label: Everyday Life

text:
Though waves may crash upon the shore,
The tide will always turn.
From depths below, there's always more—
New lessons still to learn.
label: Hope&Joy

text:
When you are old and grey and full of sleep,
And nodding by the fire, take down this book,
And slowly read, and dream of the soft look
Your eyes had once, and of their shadows deep;

How many loved your moments of glad grace,
And loved your beauty with love false or true,
But one man loved the pilgrim soul in you,
And loved the sorrows of your changing face;

And bending down beside the glowing bars,
Murmur, a little sadly, how Love fled
And paced upon the mountains overhead
And hid his face amid a crowd of stars.
label: Romantic

text:
The final, satisfying, gentle click
Of a container lid, a simple trick
That seals the freshness in, and keeps it there,
A tiny victory against the air.
A small defense against the world's decay,
To keep things good for just another day.
It is a promise, made of plastic, cheap,
Of secrets that a simple box can keep.
label: Everyday Life

text:
The public library, a silent creed,
For every lonely thought and hungry need.
The scent of paper, aging, dry, and deep,
The secrets that the leather bindings keep.
A shared and sacred, quiet, common ground,
Where not a single solitary sound
Is wasted. Just the turning of a page,
An actor stepping on a silent stage.
A cough is muffled, and a footstep's soft,
From hallowed basements to the dusty loft.
We sit together, strangers in a room,
Escaping from our own impending doom
label: Everyday Life

text:
The toast pops up, a sudden, welcome sound,
From where it was, below the heated ground.
A small machine's triumphant, final cry,
Beneath a quiet and domestic sky.
It's done its job, it's browned the bread just so,
A simple magic that we've come to know.
The smell of something, slightly burnt, and warm,
A shield against the morning’s coming storm.
label: Everyday Life

text:
The coffee mug has a small, familiar chip,
Right on the edge, exactly where I sip.
My thumb finds it, a small and jagged friend,
A story that I don't need to defend.
It isn't perfect, it is slightly cracked,
A well-loved thing, a simple, honest fact.
It holds the warmth a little longer now,
Or so I like to think, and anyhow,
It’s mine.
label: Everyday Life

text:
I am a house, but all the rooms are bare,
The furniture is gone, I know not where.
The walls are stained with where the pictures hung,
A silent testament to songs once sung.
And people knock upon the front door frame,
And call my name, my old and happy name.
They cannot know that no one's living here,
Consumed by silence, year by lonely year.
They see the structure, solid and so tall,
And do not see the ruin of it all.
I am a hollow thing, a pretty shell,
A vacant, quiet, and personal hell.
label: Misery

text:
My skin remembers where your fingers traced
The constellations on my sleeping waist.
It holds the memory, a silent art,
A map of you imprinted on my heart.
A touch so fleeting, yet it leaves a sign,
A silent vow that tells me you are mine.
label: Romantic

text:
We are just vessels, holding for a while
A certain laugh, a secret, or a smile.
We think these feelings are our own to keep,
While in the quiet of our borrowed sleep.
But they are older, they have been here long,
A timeless and recurring, human song.
We are the cup, the feeling is the wine,
And both are temporary, by design.
label: Philosophical & Reflective

text:
I wish I could show you 
How I float around everyday 
Just to fall so hard it breaks my bones 

I wish I could show you 
How I smile so wide 
My eyes try to swallow the tears I hide 

I wish I could show you 
When I walk past you my head held high 
M literally dying inside 

I wish I could 
If I could ; I really really would 
That doesn't mean I should 

My life gets Tangled in the should's
But all I want to do is 
Give in to all the could's 

But I promise 
I will let go of this 
I really would
label: Misery

text:
when i think of us i think of the lakewater
near longtown, what might not technically
constitute a lake but i prefer that word for
the open mouth of its vowel, how it called
us to its throat & held us there, in the sun,
the high points of our faces slick with light
& its arc around our shoulders, the soft
gathering of flesh around our knees,
the lone chair we found near the shore
where we took turns posing, jutting out
an eloquent hip, cackling in the bright language
of flowers for whom i downloaded an app
& learned their names: beautyberry, yarrow,
cornus florida, black-eyed susan, & you,
& you, my bright hibiscus, my every color
label: Romantic

text:
A glimpse through an interstice caught,
Of a crowd of workmen and drivers in a bar-room around the stove late of a winter night, and I unremark’d seated in a corner,
Of a youth who loves me and whom I love, silently approaching and seating himself near, that he may hold me by the hand,
A long while amid the noises of coming and going, of drinking and oath and smutty jest,
There we two, content, happy in being together, speaking little, perhaps not a word.
label: Romantic

text:
Fluorescent lights hum over aisles so wide,
With careful lists and shopping carts to guide.
A choice of apples, red or green or gold,
A story in each item to be sold.
We are the sum of what we choose to buy,
Beneath a long and ordinary sky.
label: Everyday Life

text:
O my Luve is like a red, red rose
 That’s newly sprung in June;
O my Luve is like the melody
 That’s sweetly played in tune.

So fair art thou, my bonnie lass,
 So deep in luve am I;
And I will luve thee still, my dear,
 Till a’ the seas gang dry.

Till a’ the seas gang dry, my dear,
 And the rocks melt wi’ the sun;
I will love thee still, my dear,
 While the sands o’ life shall run.

And fare thee weel, my only luve!
 And fare thee weel awhile!
And I will come again, my luve,
 Though it were ten thousand mile.
label: Romantic

text:
The swing moves though no one’s there.
The wind remembers her hum.
The teacups wait for stories
that never found an ending.
I sit and listen to the wood creak —
some love lingers
in the sound of settling.
label: Nostalgia

text:
The front door key, a jangle in the lock,
A weary clicking, like a tired clock.
The satisfying thud as it swings shut,
A line between the open world and but,
This private space, this shelter from the storm,
Familiar, safe, predictable, and warm.
label: Everyday Life

text:
The thin cool fingers of the wind
Caress your tall loveliness;
The wind kisses
Each shining strand of spun brightness
About your head
Then sends a shimmering waterfall
About the face of you;
I think the summer sun
Would be jealous of your hair,
O Golden Goddess,
Did he not know you.

As for me
I have known you through long yearning years;
Ages ago I built a home for you
Within my mind
A home where I have lived with you
So that when you came down to me
Tired of Olympus,
O Golden Goddess,
I already knew how you would be.

And yet I did not know—
For not even the clearest dream
Can equal the dazzling reality of you.
There is no way to think
The wedding of your lips with mine;
Imagination makes no magic
To match the roaring wonder
Of you close to me;
And now that you have come
My dream caught and clothed in flesh
I shall not let you go.

I shall make you part of me,
My darling,
Fundamental as heart
Primary as mind
And to you I shall become
As the blood in your veins
So that neither you nor I
Could survive
The mutilation of leaving.

Could we today
See the first atom created
Looking on as it grew into a universe
Suns burn and blossom, hurling off whole worlds
Spinning stars and planets grow tired, cold and die
Matter disintegrates
All that is become again one hapless atom
Then vanish
There still would not be time enough
To satisfy my love for you …
label: Romantic

text:
My husband has a crush on Myrna Loy,
and likes to rent her movies, for a treat.
It makes some evenings harder to enjoy.

The list of actresses who might employ
him as their slave is too long to repeat.
(My husband has a crush on Myrna Loy,

Carole Lombard, Paulette Goddard, coy
Jean Arthur with that voice as dry as wheat ...)
It makes some evenings harder to enjoy.

Does he confess all this just to annoy
a loyal spouse? I know I can’t compete.
My husband has a crush on Myrna Loy.

And can’t a woman have her dreamboats? Boy,
I wouldn’t say my life is incomplete,
but some evening I could certainly enjoy

two hours with Cary Grant as my own toy.
I guess, though, we were destined not to meet.
My husband has a crush on Myrna Loy,
which makes some evenings harder to enjoy.
label: Everyday Life

text:
She stands before them, thirty strong,
Each child a universe complete,
Some eager, some who don't belong,
Some tired, some who can't find their seat.
She knows their stories, knows their homes,
Who has enough and who has not,
Who through the neighborhood just roams,
Who's dealing with a painful lot.
But every day she still believes
That something here can make a change,
That education still achieves
What seems impossible and strange.
She stays up late to plan her lessons,
Finding ways to make them care,
About equations and geography sessions,
About metaphors and why they're there.
She celebrates each small success,
When Johnny reads without a fight,
When Maria, despite the stress,
Finally gets the answers right.
She writes encouraging notes home,
She calls the parents, good news first,
She builds the world where they can roam,
Where curiosity is nursed.
Some years are hard, some make her doubt,
The system fails, resources thin,
She wonders what it's all about,
If she can really help them win.
But then a letter comes by mail,
From someone who left years ago,
Who says: "Because of you, I didn't fail,
You helped me learn, you helped me grow."
And that's the fuel that keeps her going,
That reminds her why she's here,
That even without fully knowing,
She plants seeds year after year.
Hope is the teacher who still tries,
Who sees potential in each face,
Who helps the fallen children rise,
And fills her classroom up with grace.
Joy is the moment breakthrough comes,
When learning clicks and eyes light bright,
When futures open up for some,
Who never thought they had that right.
label: Hope&Joy

text:
“Hope” is the thing with feathers -
That perches in the soul -
And sings the tune without the words -
And never stops - at all -

And sweetest - in the Gale - is heard -
And sore must be the storm -
That could abash the little Bird
That kept so many warm -

I’ve heard it in the chillest land -
And on the strangest Sea -
Yet - never - in Extremity,
It asked a crumb - of me.
label: Hope&Joy

That left me serving out a life of time.
A loud goodbye would hurt, but it would end,
This silence is a wound that will not mend.

text:

A steady roar, a petrol-driven beast,
Whose hunger for the grass has never ceased.
It is the soundtrack of the weekend days,
A suburban and familiar haze
Of noise and duty. One more patch of green
Is forced to be acceptably clean.
label: Everyday Life

text:
The tide rises, the tide falls,

The twilight darkens, the curlew calls;

Along the sea-sands damp and brown

The traveler hastens toward the town,

And the tide rises, the tide falls.

Darkness settles on roofs and walls,

But the sea, the sea in the darkness calls;

The little waves, with their soft, white hands,

Efface the footprints in the sands,

And the tide rises, the tide falls.

The morning breaks; the steeds in their stalls

Stamp and neigh, as the hostler calls;

The day returns, but nevermore

Returns the traveler to the shore,

And the tide rises, the tide falls.
label: Hope&Joy

text:
The bus smells the same —
diesel, sweat, a hint of longing.
I sit in the seat I used to hate,
watching the same tired trees go by.
Somewhere between two stops
I see my reflection —
older, quieter, still waiting
for someone who never boarded.
label: Nostalgia

text:

Yet another punctured shells
Trying so hard to rear diamonds

Hope is a salt burning a fresh cut 
Love defines the boundaries of delusions
Happiness is sand slipping through the fist
Time is a manipulative little bitch 
Truth is a rose in the forest of thrones 

But
As the ends meet me at the horizon
I feel new
Filled with hope , love and happiness 
Where time is a friend
And truth truly mends
That's what we believe in 
That's what we call beginnings
label: Philosophical & Reflective

text:
The hammer still fits my palm.
It remembers better days.
Nails sleep in jars of rust and dust,
and silence nails the air shut.

just to feel his grip
guiding mine from memory.
label: Nostalgia

text:
The kettle screams, a whistle sharp and high,
A signal to the quiet kitchen sky.
The teabag dropped, it slowly starts to steep,
A cloud of amber, rising from the deep.
The water darkens, takes a richer shade,
A small and private ceremony made.
The steam ascends in wisps of fragrant air,
A simple antidote to all despair.
My hands wrap ‘round the warmth, a welcome friend,
A quiet moment on which I depend,
Before the world demands I start again,
To hide my thoughts from all the other men.
label: Everyday Life

text:
Act! Take Action! Be Active!

Tell me not, in mournful numbers,

Life is but an empty dream!

For the soul is dead that slumbers,

And things are not what they seem.

Life is real! Life is earnest!

And the grave is not its goal;

Dust thou art, to dust returnest,

Was not spoken of the soul.

Not enjoyment, and not sorrow,

Is our destined end or way;

But to act, that each to-morrow

Find us farther than to-day.

Art is long, and Time is fleeting,

And our hearts, though stout and brave,

Still, like muffled drums, are beating

Funeral marches to the grave.

In the world’s broad field of battle,

In the bivouac of Life,

Be not like dumb, driven cattle!

Be a hero in the strife!

Trust no Future, however pleasant!

Let the dead Past bury its dead!

Act, — act in the living Present!

Heart within, and God o’erhead!

Lives of great men all remind us

We can make our lives sublime,

And, departing, leave behind us

Footprints on the sands of time;

Footprints, that perhaps another,

Sailing o’er life’s solemn main,

A forlorn and shipwrecked brother,

Seeing shall take heart again.

Let us, then, be up and doing,

With a heart for any fate;

Still achieving, still pursuing,

Learn to labor and to wait.
label: Hope&Joy

text:
Around a table, faces glow,
Breaking bread and passing light.
Joy multiplies with all we know—
Connection makes things right.
label: Hope&Joy

text:
Hail to thee, blithe Spirit!
Bird thou never wert,
That from Heaven, or near it,
Pourest thy full heart
In profuse strains of unpremeditated art.

Higher still and higher
From the earth thou springest
Like a cloud of fire;
The blue deep thou wingest,
And singing still dost soar, and soaring ever singest.

In the golden lightning
Of the sunken sun,
O'er which clouds are bright'ning,
Thou dost float and run;
Like an unbodied joy whose race is just begun.

The pale purple even
Melts around thy flight;
Like a star of Heaven,
In the broad day-light
Thou art unseen, but yet I hear thy shrill delight,

Keen as are the arrows
Of that silver sphere,
Whose intense lamp narrows
In the white dawn clear
Until we hardly see, we feel that it is there.

All the earth and air
With thy voice is loud,
As, when night is bare,
From one lonely cloud
The moon rains out her beams, and Heaven is overflow'd.

What thou art we know not;
What is most like thee?
From rainbow clouds there flow not
Drops so bright to see
As from thy presence showers a rain of melody.

Like a Poet hidden
In the light of thought,
Singing hymns unbidden,
Till the world is wrought
To sympathy with hopes and fears it heeded not:

Like a high-born maiden
In a palace-tower,
Soothing her love-laden
Soul in secret hour
With music sweet as love, which overflows her bower:

Like a glow-worm golden
In a dell of dew,
Scattering unbeholden
Its aëreal hue
Among the flowers and grass, which screen it from the view:

Like a rose embower'd
In its own green leaves,
By warm winds deflower'd,
Till the scent it gives
Makes faint with too much sweet those heavy-winged thieves:

Sound of vernal showers
On the twinkling grass,
Rain-awaken'd flowers,
All that ever was
Joyous, and clear, and fresh, thy music doth surpass.

Teach us, Sprite or Bird,
What sweet thoughts are thine:
I have never heard
Praise of love or wine
That panted forth a flood of rapture so divine.

Chorus Hymeneal,
Or triumphal chant,
Match'd with thine would be all
But an empty vaunt,
A thing wherein we feel there is some hidden want.

What objects are the fountains
Of thy happy strain?
What fields, or waves, or mountains?
What shapes of sky or plain?
What love of thine own kind? what ignorance of pain?

With thy clear keen joyance
Languor cannot be:
Shadow of annoyance
Never came near thee:
Thou lovest: but ne'er knew love's sad satiety.

Waking or asleep,
Thou of death must deem
Things more true and deep
Than we mortals dream,
Or how could thy notes flow in such a crystal stream?

We look before and after,
And pine for what is not:
Our sincerest laughter
With some pain is fraught;
Our sweetest songs are those that tell of saddest thought.

Yet if we could scorn
Hate, and pride, and fear;
If we were things born
Not to shed a tear,
I know not how thy joy we ever should come near.

Better than all measures
Of delightful sound,
Better than all treasures
That in books are found,
Thy skill to poet were, thou scorner of the ground!

Teach me half the gladness
That thy brain must know,
Such harmonious madness
From my lips would flow
The world should listen then, as I am listening now.
label: Hope&Joy

text:
The town didn’t shrink —
I just outgrew the angles.
The same red stoplight,
the same dog asleep by the gate.
Only the silence has multiplied,
echoing where our noise once lived.
I walk home barefoot through dust
and remember believing
we’d never leave.
label: Nostalgia

text:
Let us tunnel
Through the rubble,

Through the thrum.
Let us rut through the sum

Of who we were,
Or are,

Or will be in the years to come:
A couple

Of someones
Who used to be in love.

Used to be in love.
Ho. Hum.

These days: Seem to be in hate.
Gypsum, marble, pyrite, slate.

See here. A pit of snakes.
Look there. The rock of your rages.

And I’m in a cable-cage, slinking down your shaft.
You fondle that hefty What if. . . ? as if

To hurl it. All the other holes
Are blatant hells.

A dragline scrapes our fossicked floor.
I am the ether. You are the ore.

This is the war that nobody won.
Like afterdamp collapsing a lung.

You take to swinging a pickaxe.
I take back my vamping kinks

And the pavement beneath us sinks.
This stinks. Think: In-situ leaching

But with leeches, louses,
Lampreys. Oh Spouse,

Your hard hat leaks a surfeit
Of lamp rays that’s wasted sub-surface.

A night so pitch it’s perfectly black.
A sapphire scarred by a scratch.

Sickness, health, abundance, lack.
The salt in my wound. The shirt off your back.

So our bloodcup runs empty of urge.
The metallurgy

We’re made of demands its dirge.
Our burrows diverge.

Our passages split.
Copper, silver, gravel, grit.

Am I—perhaps—alluvial?
Un-live-with-able?

A bit too simple or silty?
Only gold really ought be gilty.

And you are as cold as coal.
I am your dole, your lode,

Your carbon-flawed diamond.
All told: We drilled and hit demons.

Granite, though, is good for graves.
Granted, a mine isn’t quite a cave.

What isn’t mine, I cannot give.
label: Romantic

text:
Your laughter is a sudden, summer shower,
That breaks the heat within a single hour.
It washes over all my doubts and fears,
The sweetest music I have heard in years.
So laugh again, and let the sound cascade,
For in that joy, my own happiness is made.
label: Romantic

text:
She left her country far behind,
With one small suitcase and a dream,
She didn't know what she would find,
But nothing's ever as it seems.
The language wrapped around her tongue
Like stones she couldn't swallow down,
The words came out completely wrong,
She felt she'd never leave her frown.
The food tasted strange and bland,
The weather was too cold or hot,
She couldn't make them understand
The person that she was, was not
The person that they seemed to see,
Lost in translation, lost in space,
But still she held her dignity,
And kept hope written on her face.
She found a job, she found a room,
She found a neighbor who was kind,
Who'd also crossed an ocean's gloom,
And left her other life behind.
They shared their meals and shared their stories,
They laughed at misunderstandings,
They spoke of sorrows and past glories,
Of old homes and new landings.
And slowly, slowly, roots went down,
Into this foreign soil and air,
She learned to navigate the town,
She learned this place could also care.
Her children grew up strong and tall,
Speaking both her tongue and this,
Belonging to both, and yet to all,
A bridge between what was and is.
And when she thinks back on those days,
When everything felt hard and new,
She sees that hope found many ways
To whisper, "You will make it through."
Now she's the one who helps the others,
Who've just arrived with suitcase small,
She welcomes them like sisters, brothers,
She tells them: you will not fall.
For joy is found in starting over,
In courage to begin again,
In crossing seas to distant shores,
And finding home in foreign lands.
label: Hope&Joy

text:
What is the shape of silence, when it falls?
The quiet dust that settles on the walls.
The space between the ticking of a clock,
The patient stillness of a weathered rock.
It is not empty, it is not a void,
A secret language that we have enjoyed
And feared forever. When the noise is done,
The truth of what we are has just begun.
label: Philosophical & Reflective

text:
i miss the simple life
in the way we all do.
bringing water
from the well –
the blue one –
at every street corner.
collecting firewood
so the winter stock would last,
toasting bread on the fireplace
brushed with a garlic clove,
and salt.

i remember the signs
in windows,
people selling eggs.
creeping into the barn,
scared of spiders
and chickens,
but still collecting them,
while still warm,
and fresh.

we’d scavenge
at the edge of town –
never allowed,
but we went anyway.
swimming in ***** waters,
slick with chemicals
and gasoline,
we didn’t have allergies
to the world.
just rolled around
in grass and dirt,
not caring
what lay beneath,
or might bite.

once, we let the cat taste
the tomato soup
from my mother’s bowl,
while she was on the loo.
we snickered,
choking on laughter,
watching her savour
every spoonful.
we were partners in crime,
my brother and i.

i even miss the smell
of the old theatre.
its worn-out curtains
heavy with nerves
as we danced,
competed,
recited poems,
pretended to be
one of the great
figures of the past,
and lay on the cold,
hardwood floor,
covered in dust.

i could list
these memories for ages.
what it felt like
to be a child.
weightless.
magical.
curious,
and bright.
i wanted to grow up
too quickly.
when i should
have held on tight.
label: Nostalgia

text:
One bulb,
two mugs,
three dreams too big for rent.
The floor squeaked in rhythm
with every plan we made.
We thought the world would wait.
It didn’t.
But that room still hums
when I close my eyes.
label: Nostalgia

text:
Is time a river, flowing to the sea,
That carries on, with or without me?
Or is it just a room in which I stand,
With future and with past on either hand?
I walk its length, I think I'm moving on,
From yesterday's defeat to a new dawn.
But maybe I am still, and time's the thing
That rushes past, a bird upon the wing
label: Philosophical & Reflective

text:
The pages sigh when opened,
as if relieved to breathe.
Their words know me —
I underlined too hard,
spilled tea on page 32,
fell asleep at page 89.
They remember the version of me
that believed in every ending.
label: Nostalgia

text:
The kitchen fills with cinnamon,
With sugar, warmth, and care.
Small pleasures shared with everyone
Make love hang in the air.
label: Hope&Joy

text:
I built a future in my head for us,
Without discussion, without any fuss.
It had a porch, a dog, a climbing vine,
A world where your whole hand was holding mine.
It was a masterpiece of hope and thought,
This lovely, fragile future that I wrought.
And then you left, and with a single blow,
Destroyed a place you didn't even know.
label: Misery

text:
I found my old shoes,
their soles thin as the patience of youth.
They smell like chalk,
like the first rain on cement.
I remember running
without knowing where or why,
just to prove I could.
Now I walk carefully,
saving steps for meaning.
label: Nostalgia

text:
He picked up his first guitar at ten,
His fingers small and clumsy, weak,
The strings bit deep and hurt, but then
A chord rang out, began to speak.
The sound was magic to his ears,
Despite the buzzing, despite the pain,
He practiced through the following years,
Through sunshine days and pouring rain.
His parents said, "Have a backup plan,
Musicians rarely make their way,"
But something deeper said, "You can,
Keep playing, practice every day."
The calluses built up thick and tough,
The chords began to flow with ease,
But mastery was not enough,
He had to learn to truly please.
Not just himself but audience hearts,
To pour emotion through the strings,
To master all the subtle arts,
Of showing pain and hope and things.
That words alone could never say,
That lived between the notes he played,
He practiced hours every day,
Sometimes discouraged and dismayed.
The gigs were few and far between,
The coffee shops, the local bars,
Sometimes only five were seen,
Sitting underneath the stars.
But slowly, slowly, word got round,
About this player who could make
His instrument a living sound,
That made the listener's heart ache.
The crowds grew bigger, venues large,
He traveled far to share his gift,
He never forgot who'd been in charge—
The practice gave him all his lift.
Now when he plays to thousands strong,
Under lights and amplified,
He still remembers his first song,
Those clumsy fingers, how he tried.
And he tells the young ones who appear,
With stars reflected in their eyes,
"The journey takes you year by year,
But joy is found in each reprise."
Hope is the beginner's willing hand,
Picking up the instrument,
Despite not able to understand,
How all those hours will be spent.
Joy is the moment music flows,
From fingers to the listening air,
When you share the art and know it shows,
The love and labor planted there.
label: Hope&Joy
