Like a pathetic loser 
That 
You will see me 
Help me empty myself 
Find me ..
But I forgot 
You are at the loss of your eyes 
When it comes to me !

And yet 
M here 
Standing in this pouring rain 
Watching you 
Doing it 
All over again !!!
label: Misery

text:
You were a story that I loved to read,
A hopeful promise, and a noble deed.
I learned the chapters, memorized the lines,
Ignored the warnings and the subtle signs.
And when I reached the end, the final page,
And closed the book upon our gilded cage,
I realized the hero wasn't true,
The villain of the story, it was you.
label: Misery

text:
So, we'll go no more a roving

So late into the night,

Though the heart be still as loving,

And the moon be still as bright.

 

For the sword outwears its sheath,

And the soul wears out the breast,

And the heart must pause to breathe,

And love itself have rest.

 

Though the night was made for loving,

And the day returns too soon,

Yet we'll go no more a roving

By the light of the moon.
label: Romantic

text:
To stand beneath the vast and starry night,
Is to be humbled by the endless light.
Each star a sun, each sun a world, perhaps,
And in between, the dark and silent gaps.
And on this single grain of dust, we rage,
And write our little lines upon the page.
It is a miracle, it is absurd,
That we should have a single, hopeful word.
label: Philosophical & Reflective

text:
Who made the world?
Who made the swan, and the black bear?
Who made the grasshopper?
This grasshopper, I mean—
the one who has flung herself out of the grass,
the one who is eating sugar out of my hand,
who is moving her jaws back and forth instead of up and down—
who is gazing around with her enormous and complicated eyes.
Now she lifts her pale forearms and thoroughly washes her face.
Now she snaps her wings open, and floats away.
I don't know exactly what a prayer is.
I do know how to pay attention, how to fall down
into the grass, how to kneel down in the grass,
how to be idle and blessed, how to stroll through the fields,
which is what I have been doing all day.
Tell me, what else should I have done?
Doesn't everything die at last, and too soon?
Tell me, what is it you plan to do
with your one wild and precious life?
label: Hope&Joy

text:
The staircase creaks upon the second stair,
A sound I know, a warning it is there.
A house’s language, learned throughout the years,
That calms the nerves and settles all the fears.
Each groan and settle in the midnight dark,
A well-known, comforting, familiar mark.
label: Everyday Life

text:
The condensation on the bus-stop glass,
Watching the blurry, hurried people pass.
Each face a story that I'll never know,
A destination where I'll never go.
Just for a moment, strangers side-by-side,
With nothing but the morning rush to hide.
label: Everyday Life

text:
She stands before them, thirty strong,
Each child a universe complete,
She knows their stories, knows their homes,
Who has enough and who has not.
But every day she still believes
That something here can make a change,
She stays up late to plan her lessons,
Finding ways to make them care.
She celebrates each small success,
When Johnny reads without a fight,
When Maria, despite the stress,
Finally gets the answers right.
Some years are hard, some make her doubt,
But then a letter comes by mail,
From someone who left years ago,
Who says: "You helped me learn and grow."
Hope is the teacher who still tries,
Who sees potential in each face,
Joy is the moment breakthrough comes,
When learning clicks and eyes light bright.
label: Hope&Joy

text:
Nestled
beneath the calm of your hold

Morning’s light
Folding around us

The scent of you
Carried in the stillness

You look at me
Eyes soft, gazing

My skin remembers
where your hands have been

Silent vows
pressed
Onto my body

Colours,
Staining skin

Blues, purples and yellows

It feels so good
Everything does
It’s almost frightening

The warmth lingers
But time does not hesitate

Hold me a moment more

Let my eyes speak
The adoration
My heart keeps for you

The day pulls me away
I let it,
Unwilling
label: Romantic

text:
The market wakes before the sun,
Vendors arrive with crates and carts,
The day's commerce has just begun,
They set up stalls and play their parts.
The baker brings her sourdough bread,
Still steaming from the oven's heat,
The scent of yeast and grain has spread,
It floats along the cobbled street.
The flower seller makes bouquets,
Pink roses next to baby's breath,
Sunflowers bright as summer days,
Lilies that defeat all death.
The fruit stand glows with nature's art,
Red apples polished to a shine,
Fat peaches that could break your heart,
Purple plums and clementines.
And then the people start to come,
The market fills with life and sound,
The grandmother, the father, some
Just browsing as they walk around.
They greet each other, smile and laugh,
Exchange the news and share their days,
They buy a loaf or two, perhaps
A bunch of flowers to display.
The child tugs at her mother's hand,
Points at the pastries in a row,
The vendor doesn't need command,
He gives her one, a gift, a glow.
This is community alive,
This is connection, face to face,
This is how the heart can thrive—
In simple commerce, simple grace.
For hope is found in daily bread,
In flowers chosen just because,
In hellos and goodbyes that spread
The joy of what together does.
label: Hope&Joy

text:
I watch you read, your glasses on your nose,
The way your concentration ebbs and flows.
The little frown, the twitching of your brow,
I see a person I don't know, right now.
Lost in a world that's made of ink and thought,
A quiet battle, being bravely fought.
label: Everyday Life

text:
If you suddenly and unexpectedly feel joy, don’t hesitate.
Give in to it.
There are plenty of lives and whole towns destroyed or about to be.
We are not wise, and not very often kind.
And much can never be redeemed.
Still, life has some possibility left.
Perhaps this is its way of fighting back,
that sometimes something happens better than all the riches or power in the world.
It could be anything,
but very likely you notice it in the instant when love begins.
Anyway, that’s often the case.
Anyway, whatever it is, don’t be afraid of its plenty.
Joy is not made to be a crumb.
label: Hope&Joy

text:
Oh this rain..
This Cruel, incessant, beautiful and filling Rain...
Oh the reminders of them ...
The sweet, warm, loving, soulful...
Reminders of them..
The art they paint me into 
Spikes these infatuations 
Sometimes mild 
Other times fatal
label: Romantic

text:
The squeaky hinge upon the bedroom door,
A sound I've heard ten thousand times before.
It is a warning, it's a small hello,
It tells me all the things I need to know.
Who's coming in, who's leaving for the day,
The things that people never have to say.
I could just fix it, with a drop of oil,
And end the product of its rusty toil.
But it’s a language now.
label: Everyday Life

text:
The flickering of a fluorescent tube,
A tiny, buzzing, irritating boob-
ytrap of light. It casts a sickly pale,
And tells a nervous, hesitating tale.
It cannot choose to be completely on,
Or wait until the coming of the dawn.
It lives its life within the in-between,
The most annoying light I've ever seen.
label: Everyday Life

text:
She takes the clay between her palms,
A lump of earth, unformed and plain,
She speaks to it in whispered psalms,
And centers it again, again.
The wheel spins round beneath her hand,
She presses, pulls with gentle force,
The clay responds to her command,
And slowly rises in its course.
A wobble here, a crack appears,
She stops, collapses it, starts new,
This art requires patience over years,
And faith in what the hands can do.
The first attempts may fail and fall,
May slump or tear or lose their shape,
But she doesn't judge them much at all—
Each failure is a small escape.
From fear of trying, fear of loss,
Each ruined bowl and broken cup
Has taught her that the gain's worth cost,
That falling down means standing up.
And when at last the vase takes form,
Symmetrical and strong and true,
She knows that both the calm and storm
Have made this beauty something new.
Into the kiln it goes to face
The fire's transformative embrace,
And what emerges from that place
Has permanence and lasting grace.
We too are clay upon the wheel,
Shaped by hands we cannot see,
Sometimes broken, made to feel
The fire's intensity.
But trust the Potter knows the way,
That pressure, heat, and spinning round
Are not to harm but to convey
Us toward the beauty we have found.
label: Hope&Joy

text:
The candy shared in days of youth
Has melted in our mouths,
And left a taste so bittersweet
It lingers on the tongue.

But with each year that sweetness fades,
And bitterness we chew,
Then swallow down like sugared stones
We wish to taste anew.
label: Nostalgia

text:
How can I keep my soul in me, so that
it doesn't touch your soul? How can I raise
it high enough, past you, to other things?
I would like to shelter it, among remote
lost objects, in some dark and silent place
that doesn't resonate when your depths resound.
Yet everything that touches us, me and you,
takes us together like a violin's bow,
which draws one voice out of two separate strings.
Upon what instrument are we two spanned?
And what musician holds us in his hand?
Oh sweetest song.
label: Romantic

text:
I cried today 
At the thought of love ..
I don't know why 
But it was so hard to hold back my tears 
I don't want to come to terms 
With my fears 
Fears 
Does anyone in this universe 
Even know what they truly are ?.
Where they take us 
What they make us 
Somewhere nowhere to be found 
That far ...
It's exactly what I know 
And I think 
I know exactly what they are ..

And THEY say face the fears ..
Being completely unaware of what they rear 
How much courage one requires to gear 
To fight them only to find yourself surrounded with yet another fear ..
It's bullshit 
Listen to me 
Don't fight them away 
Keep them close to you 
Cherish them 
Love them 
Feed them on your insecurities 
And trust me 
You would be surprised to know 
What power a fear holds !!
label: Philosophical & Reflective

text:
Crisp September breeze
Carries memories
From too many years past.
Feelings unprocessed,
Echoes of uncertainty.

Promises left unkept,
Dreams and hopes
Scattered to the four winds.
And wounds untended,
Deep, ugly, gnarled.

Something in the chill,
Hauled in on the wind,
Makes the hurt return
Like an old fracture
That aches before a storm.
label: Nostalgia

text:
Buried within the heart's depth
Lie memories of the one who left
My beloved left me alone
To stare at the sky on my own

It's been ages since I saw you there
In the world outside
Or my inside
I looked for you everywhere

Dreams seen, opinions shared
Memories created, plans made
Some relations are impermanent
Like the colours of an old film fade.

Where once existed a garden,
Autumn resides my heart
Pain disguised as memories
They tear me apart

Whenever I think about you,
I loose the track of time
I feel as gloomy a poem
Without any rhyme .

My eyes flow like the sea
As I search for you
I wish I could see
My beloved beside me.
label: Nostalgia

text:
I was oblivious to this 
Until today 
Until I saw you again.
Until all of it came rushing in
Through my blood and my veins.
 I know m my criminal 
You were not even a witness, ever
I considered you my crime 
Well , I was lost in the allures of mythics
Got used to the fantasy 
Dreamt of something unreal 
And crushed it with reality 

I have to keep walking 
I know that too ...
But I was just too afraid ..
What if I left and the possibility of us 
Feels abandoned and lonely 
And eventually fade to nothing?
Now , I try to mollify 
With the nothings in you tries.
It makes me sick through the stomach 
The pain : it really hurts 
My mind throbs 
And breaths turn harsh 
Even my body knows this 
And tries to venge in the anger 
But what's the point?
You never were here , 
After all!
label: Misery

text:
The refrigerator door, a collage made
Of memories that haven't been afraid
To stick around. A magnet from the coast,
A faded picture of a much-loved host.
The number for a pizza place, askew,
A child's first drawing, rendered in bright blue.
An 'A' from a set of letters, left alone,
A story told in plastic and in stone.
It is the unintentional, messy art
Of a collective and a busy heart,
A scattered, public diary for all to see,
The history of a whole family.
label: Everyday Life

text:
Sick of it 
Sick of you 
Sick of me 
Sick of us 
Just 
So Sick 
Of every thing abt you 
Every inch of you 
Can't you just go away 
Leave me alone 
Just for one day ? 
No ? 
U might say that you 
Want to hold on to us 
Want me to understand you 
Want me to see 
How much you love me 
But 
I just don't want to 
Suffocate myself 
Down to death 
This 
Everything abt this 
Trying so hard to " make " things better 
Until the very last breath? 
Is it worth it? 
Aren't we just fooling ourselves 
And living in an illusion 
Where everything just happens to be fine 
Until and unless we are together? 
No 
It's just so false 
I wanna break free 
Free of these walls 
I want you to let me go 
Should have done this ages ago ..
But
I just hope you know 
I truly loved you 
From the get go !
label: Misery

text:
Is time a river, flowing to the sea,
That carries on, with or without me?
Or is it just a room in which I stand,
With future and with past on either hand?
I walk its length, I think I'm moving on,
From yesterday's defeat to a new dawn.
But maybe I am still, and time's the thing
That rushes past, a bird upon the wing.
label: Philosophical & Reflective

text:
The soft bedding 
The dying glow of the evening 
The slight hint of rain in the skies
The thunders and the clouds that collide
A cold gentle breeze..
That make the curtains dance in rhythms and rhymes..
This little place is my piece of us
Creating a Peace that's US

The cold nights are warm because 
I feel your body on mine..
Tangled limbs and teasing smiles..
Soft hums and whispered lullabies..
Oh my heavens..
This is the sweetest sweetest lieeee...

Your soft hair ..
My hand running through them curls..
The breaths creating a divine symphony
Soft snores ....and meaningless whines
Oh my heavens...
This is all just so divine..
label: Romantic

text:
Sometimes I feel incredibly full
Upto the brim
Overwhelmed and all over the place 
I grow out of my limits 
With every happening ..
Happening I wish I could behold
I could pause and play on my own accord
I could dance and paint an invisible art
I could live and then die a million little 
Shattering agonizing deaths
Slow and Tortuous last breaths
Hot and cold forming a dangerous mix
The purple haze gulps me whole
The haze of being loved 
The haze of being peeked deep into my very soul
The haze of being so raw and expolred
The haze of being totally yours 
Sometimes I feel incredibly full
And you empty myself form within me
No words are left unspoken
Touches unfelt
You are everything I would die for
And everything that keeps me breathing
label: Philosophical & Reflective

text:
It was many and many a year ago,

In a kingdom by the sea,

That a maiden there lived whom you may know

By the name of Annabel Lee;

And this maiden she lived with no other thought

Than to love and be loved by me.

I was a child and she was a child,

In this kingdom by the sea,

But we loved with a love that was more than love —

I and my Annabel Lee —

With a love that the wingèd seraphs of Heaven

Coveted her and me.

And this was the reason that, long ago,

In this kingdom by the sea,

A wind blew out of a cloud, chilling

My beautiful Annabel Lee;

So that her highborn kinsmen came

And bore her away from me,

To shut her up in a sepulcher

In this kingdom by the sea.

The angels, not half so happy in Heaven,

Went envying her and me —

Yes! — that was the reason (as all men know,

In this kingdom by the sea)

That the wind came out of the cloud by night,

Chilling and killing my Annabel Lee.

But our love it was stronger by far than the love

Of those who were older than we —

Of many far wiser than we —

And neither the angels in Heaven above

Nor the demons down under the sea

Can ever dissever my soul from the soul

Of the beautiful Annabel Lee;

For the moon never beams, without bringing me dreams

Of the beautiful Annabel Lee;

And the stars never rise, but I feel the bright eyes

Of the beautiful Annabel Lee;

And so, all the night-tide, I lie down by the side

Of my darling — my darling — my life and my bride,

In her sepulcher there by the sea —

In her tomb by the sounding sea.
label: Hope&Joy

text:
Lipstick is one of the very finest elixirs
It has a soft consistency
And can write on glass
Red rum is sweet
And candied
With cherries from the
Bottom of the bottle
We sip it so gracefully
I am in love again
His name is something
But who can remember
Memory is a lake
And all of that
I can’t remember
Where I left my last exercise
I was only ever sitting here
Watching the neon burn out
I have taken my red lipstick
And written Help across my chest
He looks at it so kindly
I know I am real again
I can feel the sweet bitters
Of the drink make me into a woman
I am not crying
I am writing sonnets in my mind
About all of the vacations
We will take after this one
I throw the glass against the wall
The only thing that matters in love is passion
label: Romantic

text:
The weight of grocery bags, the plastic bites
Into the fingers, turning them to whites.
A heavy burden, chosen and paid for,
From the automatic, sliding market door.
The milk is cold, the bread is soft and light,
A small collection of a future night's
Small comforts. Just a few more steps to go,
A slow and steady, miserable tow.
label: Everyday Life

text:
An old armchair, the fabric worn and thin,
Knows all the shapes the body has been in.
The right arm frayed, where nervous hands have picked,
The cushion flattened, where the cat has licked
Itself to sleep. The springs let out a groan,
A tired and familiar, weary tone.
It holds the imprint of a thousand nights
Of television's cold and flickering lights,
Of heavy sighs, and books left open wide.
There is a history held deep inside,
A silent witness, loyal to the last,
The faithful keeper of a fading past.
label: Everyday Life

text:
I used to build my walls up, stone by stone,
A fortress where I chose to be alone.
But with a single smile, you found the key,
And walked right in, dismantling all of me.
Now in the ruins, something starts to grow,
A home for two, a place I want to know.
label: Romantic

text:
The garden sleeps beneath the snow,
A blanket white and deep and cold,
Yet underneath where none can know,
The bulbs hold stories yet untold.
They wait in darkness, patient, still,
Through freezing nights and bitter days,
They trust the earth, they trust the will
Of spring to come in warming rays.
And when at last the thaw arrives,
When sun breaks through the clouded sky,
The crocuses push up their lives,
Purple and gold, they multiply.
This is the lesson gardens teach—
That nothing beautiful is lost,
That joy and hope are within reach,
No matter what the winter's cost.
label: Hope&Joy

text:
Withering leaves
Snowy streets 
Blinking lights 
And misty nights
The reddish purple glow 
Of your cheeks 
Highlighting your little nose 
Adding a glaze to your already sparkling eyes ...
Soft pink lips giving way to pearly white teeth
U look like a dream 
Showing your dimples off
I feel myself melting
Like the snow near the furnace 
Torturous and slow ..
Winters resemble you in every way 
So beautiful in yourself..
The cold seems familiar 
Has me craving for your warmth against my body
Holding me so close 
I can feel your heart beat 
Under the thick sheets ...
I am like this snow and you are the coniferous ..
Making me look beautiful 
Making me feel loved ...
And I am obsessed 
More than i can admit 
I am never letting you go 
As the coniferous does for the snow ..
label: Romantic

text:
When the room is empty,
and the people have left
and you’re waiting, wondering,
what will come next?

A haven of memories,
long phone calls and late-night dances
hard work and parades of tears
then left with hardly a glance.

So many firsts
trapped in one room
the thoughts and the feeling,
stuck in its loom.

It’s no longer yours,
the decorations pulled down,
bare and barren just like when you moved in,
might never have left your hometown.
label: Nostalgia

text:
This night is exceptionally cold
It rattles my bones 
Has me craving for a warm embrace 
Has me thinking about the friends
The moments and then the emptiness 
Its so lonely around here 
A deserted forest 
An iced glacier 
Long throny roads 
Unaware of the end
Hanging just to be there 
Struggling hard for their existence 
And trying to cope 
The slightest light shimmers 
Giving an ounce of hope 
Then the colors fill the sky 
Dark and light 
The drops fall 
Abandoned by the clouds 
Leaving thunder along their wake 
The forest deserted 
Dances with the soft touches of the rain 
The glacier melts 
Never want to be here again 
I would find my rain 
That would make me feel home 
Who wouldn't leave me 
Feeling like shit 
And Pathetically alone!!
label: Misery

text:
Are all our choices truly ever made,
Or just a part within a play, long-played?
We think we choose the path, the hill, the bend,
The start, the middle, and the final end.
But maybe we are reading from a page,
An actor on a pre-determined stage.
And free will is the art of reading well,
The lines within our own specific hell.
label: Philosophical & Reflective

text:
My soul is now a cup, with a small crack,
From which the will to live will not come back.
You can pour kindness, or a gentle word,
The sweetest sentiment I've ever heard.
But it all leaks out, a slow and steady drain,
A useless effort, and a wasted pain.
It cannot hold the joy, it cannot keep
A single promise from the broken deep.
label: Misery

text:
Walking down the memory lane 
I often visit the places 
The times when 
Everything seemed to be a complete mess 
Where 
I got drunk on all the negatives 
And allergic of the positives 
Where 
I preferred the deeps 
Feared the sunshines 
Drowned into the lows
And pitied the highs 
Hope made me feel empty and alone 
Giving up seemed the best way out 
Then 
I remember the mirror I was shown 
The reason for me to try 
And hope 
To be with all the positives 
And love the highs 
I saw me 
The me 
Who was evolved 
Who is loved 
And knows how to love 
Not anyone else 
But his ownself
label: Philosophical & Reflective

text:
More than a catbird hates a cat,
Or a criminal hates a clue,
Or the Axis hates the United States,
That's how much I love you.

I love you more than a duck can swim,
And more than a grapefruit squirts,
I love you more than gin rummy is a bore,
And more than a toothache hurts.

As a shipwrecked sailor hates the sea,
Or a juggler hates a shove,
As a hostess detests unexpected guests,
That's how much you I love.

I love you more than a wasp can sting,
And more than the subway jerks,
I love you as much as a beggar needs a crutch,
And more than a hangnail irks.

I swear to you by the stars above,
And below, if such there be,
As the High Court loathes perjurious oaths,
That's how you're loved by me.
label: Romantic

text:
I watch the ants, a line of hurried black,
Along the weathered window-frame's small crack.
They have a purpose, resolute and clear,
Ignoring all the giants living here.
One lifts a crumb, a burden twice its size,
A tiny hero in my watching eyes.
There is no chaos, just a steady pace,
A mission for their tiny, teeming race.
They work as one, a singular machine,
The most efficient army ever seen,
And I just watch, and wonder what it's for,
This endless, patient, microscopic war.
label: Everyday Life

text:
The kitchen sink, with dishes piled up high,
Beneath the patient, non-judgmental sky.
The water's warm, the soap creates a haze,
A quiet end to one of many days.
Each plate is cleaned, a small and simple grace,
Putting the day back in its proper place.
label: Everyday Life

text:
The old radio clicks to life,
crackling through static and ghosts.
Every melody is a photograph,
every note a name I lost.
Mom hums along in another room,
her voice a bridge between centuries.
I stay quiet —
some memories shouldn’t be disturbed
once they start to sing.
label: Nostalgia

text:
I love your lips when they’re wet with wine
And red with a wild desire;
I love your eyes when the lovelight lies
Lit with a passionate fire.
I love your arms when the warm white flesh
Touches mine in a fond embrace;
I love your hair when the strands enmesh
Your kisses against my face.

Not for me the cold, calm kiss
Of a virgin’s bloodless love;
Not for me the saint’s white bliss,
Nor the heart of a spotless dove.
But give me the love that so freely gives
And laughs at the whole world’s blame,
With your body so young and warm in my arms,
It sets my poor heart aflame.

So kiss me sweet with your warm wet mouth,
Still fragrant with ruby wine,
And say with a fervor born of the South
That your body and soul are mine.
Clasp me close in your warm young arms,
While the pale stars shine above,
And we’ll live our whole young lives away
In the joys of a living love.
label: Romantic

text:
The hiss of tires on the dampened street,
A bittersweet and lonely, soft retreat
Of sound. The world is washed in shades of grey,
At the conclusion of another day.
The streetlights bleed their halos on the ground,
A quiet, melancholy world I've found.
And in the car, the wipers keep the time,
A steady and a metronomic crime
Against the silence.
label: Everyday Life

text:
I wear a mask of smiles, a thin disguise,
To hide the barren landscape in my eyes.
I learn the lines, I play the cheerful part,
With carefully rehearsed and hollow art.
And people think I'm fine, they think I'm strong,
They do not hear the melancholy song
That plays inside, a sad and constant tune,
Beneath the bright and unforgiving noon.
label: Misery

text:
When death comes, final and novel—
the trick cul-de-sac or burst dam, a door
that opening erases or shutting disappears—
I want to watch the filmstrip once more.

I want to see the caterpillar, his whole body
a fat neck, rolling hills and horripilation.
I want to remember how, every time
he dropped from some leaf to my arm,
we appalled each other. I want to remember
how, every time, astonishment
made us the same.

When death comes, I want to watch,
reel-to-reel, my unrealized fears
yielding to what I never thought to dread:
meningitis, oily rags.

I want to flip back to the times
my name came up lucky
in the raffle of who-gets-to-see-herons.

All my life I was a bride married to amazement: 
every sickness an affront, every peach
a geode. All my life: bowled over by every hell—
fresh, stale, it didn’t matter—
and all my life blown about, a feather
on the breath of God.

When it is over, I want to say: it was a requited love.

But probably, even then, I will not know
whether I have passed my life as surprise’s adept
or her dupe.

Probably, even then, I will only
be sure of  falling asleep
beside my strange bedfellow, strange.
label: Hope&Joy

text:
I always thought why people try 
To hurt themselves?
I mean it's never worth it anyways 
Is it? 
Back then I always stood away 
From the decorated scars hanging around 
I used to point at them and then 
Would just turn away 
Disgusted as always
But it's never about the scars is it?
Be it on the body 
Or deep inside 
It's about the pain 
And then the gushing relief 
Relief of finding the outsource 
Then appear the cuts , the marks 
The covered body 
Of decorated scars..
But still it's a coward's way out
Then you say I turned into one somehow
Well you would be surprised 
No one's going to applaud for your decorations ever
And the pain
Still lingers forever
label: Philosophical & Reflective

text:
What will be left of us when we are gone?
A fading echo, carried on the dawn.
A ripple from a stone, that spreads so wide,
With nowhere left for it to run or hide,
Until it finds the shore, a gentle sigh,
And then is gone, beneath an empty sky.
Is that enough? A momentary trace?
To prove we occupied this time and place?
label: Philosophical & Reflective

text:
The way the steam fogs up the bathroom mirror,
And makes the known world so much less clearer.
Your own reflection is a blurry ghost,
The thing you recognize, and fear the most.
You wipe a circle, with a single hand,
And see a single eye, in a strange land.
The rest is hidden, in a cloudy haze,
For the remainder of your morning days.
label: Everyday Life

text:
The redolence of rose and new road.
The attar of grass, recently cut.
The fish-whiff of anchovy.
The spinning NYC glizzys in a tank of murky water.
The famous Coney Island corn dog on a pale wooden stick.
The black mud of Slovakia, drinking  Tatratea, Borovička, Slivovica, Zlatý Bažant.
The misted pastry shop windows of  Vienna.
The rød pølse in Copenhagen’s main square, Rådhuspladsen.
The grilled weisswurst of East Berlin.
The straw baskets of washed-rind cheese of the Piedmont.
The spill of Barolo, tar and roses.
The shelled seafood from the Venetian lagoon.
The delicate, damp fish of  Lima in lime and cilantro.
The numbing citrus of mock peppercorns from Chengdu.
The tang of fermented pickle.
The ginger off  boiling wok oil.
The blue smoke of  lamb at night on the braziers of  Tangier.
The steam of  Ramadan harira at midnight in Tangier’s Socco Grande.
The sfenj rolling in boiling oil, morning in Fez, powdered sugar.
The finale of everything that coheres.
label: Hope&Joy

text:

At other times

If it were a dream ..
But reality pinches you hard 
On your cheek ; 
It's something unavoidable 
It's so bothersome that you are scared ..
"What if I face it ..
And then turn into a freak?"
That is what you ask yourself 
Everytime you run ..
I wonder does all of this make things better? 
Down to the ashes we burn ..
We burn , burn and keep on burning 
Until it has destroyed every ounce of hope!
Then we go out ..
And search for an embrace 
Which is willing to get burnt ...
To your surprise 
It soothes your wound 
And heals them from the core 
You discover that you are as new as you were before ..
Just ready to burn more
label: Everyday Life

text:
A single coin, a penny on the street,
Ignored by all the quickly passing feet.
It's heads-up, so it should be lucky, right?
Reflecting back the sun's indifferent light.
But no one stops to pick it up, to see,
A tiny piece of currency, for free.
It waits there, for a child's excited hand,
Or to be buried in the dust and sand.
label: Everyday Life

text:
We painted it yellow,
thinking joy was permanent.
It leaned for years
against the garden wall,
a relic of speed and freedom.
Now rust has turned the joy
a darker shade of memory.
label: Nostalgia

text:

label: Romantic

text:
The buzz-saw snarled and rattled in the yard

And made dust and dropped stove-length sticks of wood,

Sweet-scented stuff when the breeze drew across it.

And from there those that lifted eyes could count

Five mountain ranges one behind the other

Under the sunset far into Vermont.

And the saw snarled and rattled, snarled and rattled,

As it ran light or had to bear a load.

And nothing happened: the day was all but done.

Call it a day, I wish they might have said

To please the boy by giving him the half-hour

That a boy counts so much when saved from work.

His sister stood beside him in her apron

To tell them ‘Supper.’ At the word, the saw,

As if to prove saws knew what supper meant,

Leaped out at the boy’s hand, or seemed to leap —

He must have given the hand. However, it was,

Neither refused the meeting. But the hand!

The boy’s first outcry was a rueful laugh,

As he swung toward them holding up the hand

Half in appeal, but half as if to keep

The life from spilling. Then the boy saw all —

Since he was old enough to know, big boy

Doing a man’s work, though a child at heart —

He saw all spoiled. ‘Don’t let him cut my hand off —

The doctor, when he comes. Don’t let him, sister!’

So. But the hand was gone already.

The doctor put him in the dark of ether.

He lay and puffed his lips out with his breath.

And then — the watcher at his pulse took fright.

No one believed. They listened at his heart.

Little — less — nothing! — and that ended it.

No more to build on there. And they, since they

Were not the one dead, turned to their affairs.
label: Hope&Joy

text:
You are a song stuck on a sad repeat,
A bitter melody that was so sweet.
I hear it in the silence of my car,
No matter where I go, no matter how far.
I try to change the station, but the sound
Is everywhere, above and underground.
It is the soundtrack to my own despair,
A tune that follows me just everywhere.
label: Misery

text:
The traffic light, from red to green to gold,
A story that is getting very old.
We stop and wait, we hurry and we press,
A shared and silent, synchronized duress.
A simple system, holding us in line,
A brief and frustrating, but common sign.
label: Everyday Life

text:
A single weed, determined and so green,
Breaks through a crack of concrete, unforeseen.
No garden here, no softly tended ground,
Just dust and debris scattered all around.
And yet it grows, a small and stubborn thing,
A silent testament to life in spring.
label: Everyday Life

text:
Sometimes I watch you when you do not see,
Just going on about your day, carefree.
A simple gesture, how you sip your tea,
And a wave of adoration washes over me.
How did I live before this perfect view?
How did I ever breathe, not loving you?
label: Romantic

text:
The street is black and shining from the rain,
A fractured mirror, showing all the pain
And all the beauty of the city's light.
The neon signs, impossibly bright,
Are bleeding, upside down, across the ground,
A silent, vibrant, and inverted town.
The hiss of tires on the dampened street,
A smell of wet concrete, a bittersweet
And earthy perfume after heat and dust.
A world reborn from atmospheric rust,
Where every puddle holds a captured star,
No matter where or who or what you are.
label: Everyday Life

label: Everyday Life

text:
Is truth a rock, a thing that you can hold,
A perfect story, meant to be retold?
Or is it just a candle in the night,
That casts a single, solitary light,
And in the darkness, shadows leap and play,
And show the world in a completely different way.
My shadow-truth is just as real to me,
As the small flame that you have claimed to see.
Perhaps the truth is not the light we find,
But the specific shadows left behind.
label: Philosophical & Reflective

text:
The garden sleeps beneath the snow,
A blanket white and deep and cold,
Yet underneath where none can know,
The bulbs hold stories yet untold.
They wait in darkness, patient, still,
Through freezing nights and bitter days,
They trust the earth, they trust the will
Of spring to come in warming rays.
And when at last the thaw arrives,
When sun breaks through the clouded sky,
The crocuses push up their lives,
Purple and gold, they multiply.
The tulips follow, red and bright,
The daffodils in yellow dress,
They celebrate the end of night,
They dance in spring's sweet tenderness.
This is the lesson gardens teach—
That nothing beautiful is lost,
That joy and hope are within reach,
No matter what the winter's cost.
So when your heart feels frozen through,
Remember bulbs beneath the ground,
Have faith that life will spring in you,
And color will again be found.
label: Hope&Joy

text:
Grief is a room, and I have lost the key,
There is no window here that I can see.
The air is thick with all the words unsaid,
A heavy blanket on an empty bed.
The clock has stopped, the hands refuse to move,
Stuck in the moment I could not disprove.
And time outside goes on, a muffled beat,
The sound of someone else’s passing feet.
label: Misery

text:
The low, persistent humming of the fridge,
The quiet keeper of the kitchen's edge.
It is the only sound when night is deep,
A steady watch it promises to keep.
The sound of stillness, in a sleeping house,
As quiet as a solitary mouse.
label: Everyday Life

text:
If I had the chance to relive one part of my life, 
I’d do things differently with us. 
I’d say “I love you” more often than I did, 
and press a kiss to your lips the night we met.

I’d bring you flowers, yellow roses 
just to bring warmth into your life, 
because you told me you suffered too much. I’d hold you tighter if I had the chance, 
rest my head on your chest more, not less. 

I’d place a kiss on the tip of your nose, 
and maybe we’d get married 
and dance to your favourite song.

I’d tell my mother you were the one.
 I’d declare to her there was nothing but us. 
I’d say something better
than “we’re too young,” 
because it was such a silly thing to keep us apart.
 But we were just kids, stupidly in love 
and what could we do 
when we were only thirteen years old?

If time were kind enough to give me another moment, 
I’d learn how to listen to the silences
 hidden between your words. 
I’d keep every secret you trusted me with
 and guard it like a treasure.
 I’d walk beside you longer, even when the road got dark, 
and I’d whisper your name like a promise
I meant to keep.
But time doesn’t wait, and the past stays where it belongs.

 All I can do now is carry your memory
like sunlight in my hands, 
forgive the children we once were, 
and thank you for teaching me 
what love felt like the first time.

And if some distant evening
 our paths should cross again, 
I’ll smile at you softly 
and hope you’ll know without words
 that I always loved you 
then, now, and in every life 
where I get another chance.

And until that day,
 I’ll plant yellow roses in gardens that aren’t ours, 
watch them bloom and wither without you. I’ll hear our songs in empty rooms, 
and dance alone under a sky 
that keeps its stars to itself. 
It won’t change the past, 
but it will remind me gently 
how something so young 
could still ache like forever.

I’ll walk down streets that feel like echoes, where every shadow holds a memory of your face.
 Sometimes I’ll whisper your name into the wind 
just to feel it leave my mouth again. Sometimes I’ll close my eyes
 and picture the life we might have built
 not to torture myself, but to keep it real
 for a few more heartbeats.

And when the seasons turn, 
I’ll stand at the edge of winter, 
holding a single yellow rose, 
knowing it will never reach you but still lifting it toward the sky  as if it might.

 Because even if we never meet again, somewhere in the quiet between my breaths you’re still there, thirteen and smiling, 
and I’m still reaching for you.
label: Nostalgia

text:
The child’s chalk drawing on the pavement square,
A crooked sun with brilliant yellow hair.
A house with smoke that curls up from the top,
A world of wonders that will never stop.
The colors, vibrant on the dusty grey,
Will not be here to see another day.
The next rain comes, and it will wash away
This temporary, beautiful display.
But for this moment, in the afternoon,
Beneath the silent and observant moon
That’s yet to come, the art is pure and true,
A masterpiece of pink and white and blue.
label: Everyday Life

text:
I nailed the windows shut, I locked the door,
So that your ghost can't haunt me anymore.
But you are not a spirit, not a guest,
You are a cancer growing in my breast.
You are the phantom ache in my left hand,
A language only I can understand.
There is no lock, no key, no bolted gate
To keep you out. It is already too late.
label: Misery

text:
Drunk, we walked west to the ocean,
drop soup and sake,
sloshing in our guts.

You would marry in twenty days.
I stayed close,
swallowing the words
that would’ve ruined it all.

In seven years,
I will have a son.
You will bury yours.
We will wonder - quietly -
if souls can be traded,
if grief moves
like a current
between blood that is not blood.

The tide was electric,
a woman waded in,
cupped bioluminescence
like an ember from the deep.

We stood apart from the others,
two men
bone-wet and wind-bit,
trying to scratch our names
into blue light,
signatures gone
before the next wave came.

I never told you the future.
I let the dark reclaim our feet.
You laughed,
drunk and perfect,
and I looked away
as the sea
turned the sand
back to stone.
label: Nostalgia

text:

text:

Replaying harsher words

so strong

text:
I want to memorize the precise shade
Of brown that your eyes turn in evening's fade.
I want to learn the cadence of your speech,
Keep every little lesson you can teach.
My life's a study, and the subject's you,
In everything you are, and all you do.
label: Romantic

text:
Moments filled with joy,
Float away like pink balloons,
Never to be seen again.

I miss those pink balloons,
The type of pink that looks terrible on curtains,
but looks good on you.

I mourn the lack of pink,
I try to find a new colour,
My new favourite colour.

It might take a while till I find it,
but I'll always remember those pink balloons.
label: Nostalgia

text:
You are invulnerable. Didn’t they deliver
(those forces that control your destiny)
the certainty of dust? Couldn’t it be
your irreversible time is that river
in whose bright mirror Heraclitus read
his brevity? A marble slab is saved
for you, one you won’t read, already graved
with city, epitaph, dates of the dead.
And other men are also dreams of time,
not hardened bronze, purified gold. They’re dust
like you; the universe is Proteus.
Shadow, you’ll travel to what waits ahead,
the fatal shadow waiting at the rim.
Know this: in some way you’re already dead.
label: Hope&Joy

text:
The last one left to turn the office lights
Away from all the half-done days and nights.
The monitors are blank, a row of black,
The empty chairs that never will look back.
A quiet hum from some forgotten fan,
The silent witness to a failed-out plan.
The day is over, done, and put to rest,
Another long and inconclusive test.
label: Everyday Life

text:
the expanse of hallway outside my hotel door
seems to go on forever
the space seems to embody an otherworldly feeling
between our world and some other
indescribable place
is it comforting or claustrophobic?
I used to visit our small town mall
when I was young, it was bustling with life
it had a movie theater
with endearingly tacky Electra-Dye carpets,
an arcade, and a Borders bookstore.
years passed, and the place became a husk.
movie theaters are on the decline,
and the bookstore went bankrupt.
malls are shutting down all over the world
due to the popularity of online shopping
and digital streaming.
movie theater architecture no longer looks like
an odyssey into space,
but a hotel lobby with neutral colors.
humanity left it all behind.
we gave these spaces life with our humanity.
the liminal spaces were alive with the
frenetic energy of living.
they were meant to be inhabited.
I visited our local mall.
there were only a few other people.
it felt like I wasn't supposed to see it that way— devoid of life, devoid of the meaning
humanity described it.
it became a relic of the past.
I wandered the hallways
and saw the movie posters they displayed.
the showings were from seasons before,
and they were peeling off the walls.
it felt like I was left behind too.
liminal zones are really the state in between
the past and the present,
nostalgia and the modern age.
the walls were just walls.
the carpets just carpets.
but my memories gave it meaning.
if birth is the beginning and death is the end
life is the liminal space.
label: Nostalgia

text:
I find a note inside my winter coat,
A shopping list, a hastily-made boat
Of words. "Milk, bread, and coffee," I can see,
A ghost of who I was, or used to be.
I don't remember writing it at all,
Sometime last winter, probably the fall.
A simple message from a forgotten day,
That I will likely just put back away.
label: Everyday Life

text:
The oak tree stands three hundred years,
Has weathered drought and flood and storm,
Has seen the happiness and tears,
Of all who've sat beneath its form.
It started as an acorn small,
Dropped by its mother long ago,
Who could have guessed it would grow tall,
Who could have known how it would grow?
The first year it was just a shoot,
So tender, green, and thin and frail,
It worked to dig down deep its root,
Through soil and rock and clay and shale.
The rabbits nibbled at its leaves,
The deer came by and browsed its tips,
But something deep inside believes,
In reaching high and sinking ships.
Of roots down to the water far,
Through decades it kept pushing on,
Surviving drought and fire's scar,
Regrowing when its leaves were gone.
And year by year it added rings,
Each circle marking time survived,
Through hard times and through better things,
Through all the years that it had thrived.
It sheltered generations now,
The children climbed its sturdy limbs,
The lovers carved into its bough,
Their initials and their whims.
The old folks sat beneath its shade,
And told their stories to the young,
About the memories they'd made,
About the songs that they had sung.
And when the storms came fierce and wild,
The oak tree bent but would not break,
Its flexibility was styled,
By wisdom that the years could make.
It dropped its leaves when winter called,
Stood bare and patient through the cold,
And never once felt appalled,
For spring would come as it'd been told.
And sure enough the buds appeared,
The leaves unfurled in green so bright,
The tree had nothing ever feared,
For it had learned to hold on tight.
To hope that's rooted deep and strong,
To joy that comes from standing still,
From knowing that you do belong,
Exactly here upon this hill.
We can learn from the oak tree's way,
Of patience, growth, and steady grace,
Of bending in the storm but stay,
Of keeping deep roots in our place.
Of shedding what no longer serves,
Of trusting spring will come again,
Of holding steady to the curves,
That life will throw at us, and then—
Of growing slowly, year by year,
Of adding depth and strength and rings,
Of sheltering those we hold dear,
And celebrating simple things.
label: Hope&Joy

text:
Who is the person that you are in dreams?
A braver soul, or so it often seems.
A silent hero, or a frightened child,
Emotional, irrational, and wild.
And when you wake, you put him back in place,
And wear your calm and ordinary face.
But which one is the actor, which is true?
The one who dreams, or the one who sees it through?
label: Philosophical & Reflective

text:
We think a choice is made, a door is shut,
A simple, clean, and well-defined clear cut.
But every path we did not choose to take,
Still travels with us, for our own heart's sake.
A ghostly version of the self we spurned,
A lesson that was never truly learned.
And in the quiet, you can hear them call,
The people we became by losing all
The other people we were meant to be,
A silent and invisible debris.
label: Philosophical & Reflective

text:
I remember the YOU : you 
Used to be ;
Vulnerable but fixed easily !
Blinded by love ;
And held by trust ;
Open and transparent !
Filled with Innocence;
An unimaginable beauty 
With a pure heart !!!

If you ask me ;
That was terrifying ..
What if someone broke u?
What if u Scatter in such a way ,
That I won't be able to pick up the pieces?
What if I loose the YOU in you?
What if ..
U shut the every damn thing u give any fucks about 
Including ME !?
That 
Is
SCARY!!!

But now I see YOU; 
Now you are ;
Strong and unbreakable ;
Weak but not in the way ,
That would hurt you!
You let go 
The things you can't control!!
And keep the ones 
You cherish 
Blended with your soul !!
You speak your mind ;
You have really become 
That ONE OF A KIND !!
I swear 
I am bound to become 
Blind ;
By the light you carry 
Without even trying !!

And if you ask me;
I still see YOU ..
The way you want to be seen ;
And the way you don't ..
And I know now if you break,

label: Philosophical & Reflective

text:
love is more thicker than forget
more thinner than recall
more seldom than a wave is wet
more frequent than to fail

it is most mad and moonly
and less it shall unbe
than all the sea which only
is deeper than the sea

love is less always than to win
less never than alive
less bigger than the least begin
less littler than forgive

it is most sane and sunly
and more it cannot die
than all the sky which only
is higher than the sky
label: Romantic

text:
Once there was a prince 
Who didn't know nothing 
But love and happiness 
He carried these heavy loads
Wherever and whenever he went ..

In a parallel universe 
Existed a princess 
She knew everything 
Except love and happiness 
She didn't set a foot out of her castle 
And liked to be trapped 
In the dark..

She loved the atrocities of the past 
Believed that they existed forever and were there to last. ..

He loved the brightest side of life 
He was so bright 
That he overshadowed the stars 
Like the sun does on a bright sunny day 

She was a new moon night 
While he was the break of dawn ..

Do always 
The prince and the princess need to coincide ? 
Like a bright and warm day falls into the cold and dark embrace of a night? 
Are they always destined to complete each other? 

Can't just they be incomplete forever ? 
Becoz for something that's left undone 
There is always hope ..
And it continues till forever 
Or it will perish in one milli second ..
Who knows 
It may stay 
Or might go away ...
label: Philosophical & Reflective

text:
If you can keep your head when all about you

Are losing theirs and blaming it on you,

If you can trust yourself when all men doubt you,

But make allowance for their doubting too;

If you can wait and not be tired by waiting,

Or being lied about, don’t deal in lies,

Or being hated, don’t give way to hating,

And yet don’t look too good, nor talk too wise:

If you can dream — and not make dreams your master;

If you can think — and not make thoughts your aim;

If you can meet with Triumph and Disaster

And treat those two impostors just the same;

If you can bear to hear the truth you’ve spoken

Twisted by knaves to make a trap for fools,

Or watch the things you gave your life to, broken,

And stoop and build ’em up with worn-out tools:

If you can make one heap of all your winnings

And risk it on one turn of pitch-and-toss,

And lose, and start again at your beginnings

And never breathe a word about your loss;

If you can force your heart and nerve and sinew

To serve your turn long after they are gone,

And so hold on when there is nothing in you

Except the Will which says to them: ‘Hold on!’

If you can talk with crowds and keep your virtue,

Or walk with Kings — nor lose the common touch,

If neither foes nor loving friends can hurt you,

If all men count with you, but none too much;

If you can fill the unforgiving minute

With sixty seconds’ worth of distance run,

Yours is the Earth and everything that’s in it,

And — which is more — you’ll be a Man, my son!
label: Hope&Joy

text:
My mind is now a courtroom, and I stand
Accused of crimes I do not understand.
The prosecutor looks and sounds like me,
And shows the jury all my frailty.
The judge is me, the witness is me too,
There's nothing that a person here can do.
The verdict's in, it's guilty, that's the plea,
And I am sentenced to a life of me.
label: Misery

text:
I have a garden, but the soil is sour,
And nothing grows there, not a single flower.
I plant the seeds of hope, I water them with tears,
And have been doing so for all these years.
But only weeds of bitterness will sprout,
And wrap their thorny arms and choke them out.
I should just give it up, and let it be
A monument to all my misery.
label: Misery

text:
The first fruit I ever stole
came from an old man I don’t know the name of.
I know he couldn’t move
from his La-Z-Boy by the front window.
I know how his gravelly voice boomed across the yard
as he scolded me for taking peaches from his tree.
I don’t know why he cared.
I know my sister would smile when I brought them home.
And I know my brother had this habit—
biting only one side
until he reached the pit.
I don’t know what happened to the old man,
but I know the peaches started something bigger.
I know I later became a thief—
but also had this habit
of giving people fruit when they’d come over.
I don’t know if the old man knew my name,
or if he just called me the brat who stole his peaches.
I know they cut down that peach tree
when I was in ninth grade.
And I know
I’ve never had a peach so sweet
label: Nostalgia

text:
We used to trap fireflies in jars
and promise not to let them die.
Now I trap moments instead,
tiny, glowing, breathless —
and break every promise again.
label: Nostalgia

text:
It's not the screaming, not the angry cries,
But the indifferent coldness in your eyes.
It's not the slamming of the kitchen door,
But the silence when you don't care anymore.
This quiet apathy, this gentle hate,
Has sealed the lock upon our closing gate.
A loud goodbye would be a kinder thing
Than the slow, silent poison that you bring.
label: Misery

text:
I’d break into our old family home
if it was still standing tall.
Electrical cords and floorboards;
that I would finely comb.
Searching for traces of us; big or small.

I should’ve taken the tub,
and the dryer was brand new.
I know they ripped up each stump and stub
and the yellow roses the year they finally grew.

This is just a missing piece,
this is where I used to live.
Memorized the trees and the streets,
and the faces I would greet,
to go see it again; what I wouldn’t give.

I’d break into our old family house,
if it was still standing tall.
As I dowse, no sound or a mouse,
was it ever even there at all?

Why did they lose the shed?
Why did they cover the lot?
It looks better in my head
than the day it was finally bought.

This is just a missing past,
this is where we used to coexist,
those rare family moments that I miss.
They’re now lost to the abyss,
I don’t remember the last.

I’d go back in time but
I wouldn’t want to impose.
Truth is the door is shut,
realize that road is closed.

I’m sad I don’t live there anymore,
I grew up; have my own walls and own floor.
A woman who loves me and her I adore,
but these thoughts still wash ashore.
label: Nostalgia

text:
You tell me that you love me, then you leave.
You say you're sorry, what should I believe?
You are the warmth, you are the freezing cold,
A story that is getting very old.
You offer me a flower, then a thorn,
And I'm the fool who's waiting for the morn
That never comes. This isn't love, I know,
It is a garden where the poisons grow.
label: Misery
Romantic

text:
I watch the happy people passing by,
Beneath a hopeful and untroubled sky.
They hold their hands, they laugh, they are so loud,
A single person in a walking crowd.
And I'm a statue, watching them all go,
A secret that they will not ever know.
I am the quiet, uninvited guest,
Who fails the world’s ridiculously simple test.
label: Misery

text:
It’s not the lightning, not the crashing storm,
But just the simple feeling of your form.
The steady rhythm of your sleeping breath,
A quiet shield against all thoughts of death.
You are the anchor in my drifting sea,
The calm, the peace, the certainty.
label: Romantic

text:
I’ve replayed every word inside my head,
The conversation on our final bed.
I change my lines, I try a different tone,
A different ending, not to be alone.
I practice it a thousand times a day,
The perfect, magic thing I meant to say.
But it is just a ghost, a useless art,
This frantic, stupid practice of the heart.
label: Misery
Misery
